English: Date: ???  Time: ??? Location: ???

Japanese: プルルル‥‥‥‥ プルルル‥‥‥‥
English: *RIIING*... *RIIING*...

Japanese: （だ、だれだ‥‥こんな時間に）
English: (Who could that be at this time of night...?)

Japanese: ‥‥はい。こちら、ミツルギ‥‥
English: ...Yes? Edgeworth speaking...

Japanese: ナニしてんだよ御剣！ サッサと出ろよな！
English: Edgey! Get up! It's an emergency!

Japanese: ‥‥や、矢張か‥‥。 今、何時だと思っている？
English: ...Huh? Larry...? Do you know what time it is?

Japanese: オレは矢張じゃねえ！ 天流斎　マシスだッ！
English: It's not "Larry"! It's "Laurice"! Laurice Deauxnim!

Japanese: （どうやら、きわめて悪質なユメを 見ているらしいな、私は‥‥）
English: (This is nothing more than a terrible nightmare... I'll just roll over and...)

Japanese: ま、待て！　切るなって！ ‥‥タイヘンなんだよ！
English: W-Wait! Don't hang up! ...It's an emergency!

Japanese: 成歩堂がッ！　アイツ‥‥ 落っこちやがったんだよ！
English: It's Nick! H-He... He took a really nasty spill!

Japanese: ‥‥彼が落ちるのは、 今に始まったことではなかろう。
English: Well, it wouldn't be the first time, so...

Japanese: 試験のハナシじゃねえよ！ イノチがあぶねえんだって！
English: I'm not joking! His life is in danger!

Japanese: な、なんだと‥‥！ ‥‥どういうことだ！
English: Wh-What...!? What happened!? Tell me!

Japanese: トコトン運のワルいヤツだからな。 もう、死んでるかもしれねえ。
English: Talk about a guy with bad luck! He may already be dead!

Japanese: とにかく‥‥すぐ帰ってきてくれ！ オマエしか、いねえんだよお‥‥
English: Anyway, you've got to come back! You're the only one that can help!

Japanese: オレの‥‥オレの、 あやめちゃんがああ‥‥
English: My Iris... My beautiful Iris! She needs help...!

Japanese: よくわからんが‥‥わかった。 ‥‥すぐ、そちらへ向かう。
English: Alright. I don't know what's going on, but... I'll be there as soon as I can.

Japanese: オレ‥‥留置所にいるからさあ！ 今すぐ来てくれえええええええ！
English: I-I'm at the detention center! Please! Hurryyyyyy!

Japanese: （あの国をはなれて、１年‥‥）
English: (It's been one year since I left that country...)

Japanese: （しばらく会うこともあるまいと 思っていたのだが‥‥）
English: (I thought I wouldn't have to see him again for a while...)

Japanese: （なつかしい対面とはいかない ようだな‥‥いつもどおり‥‥）
English: (Sounds like it won't be a pretty reunion... As if I expected anything to change.)

Japanese: ２月　８日　午後　２時１９分 留置所　面会室
English: February 8, 2:19 PM Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: おせェぞ御剣！ ナニやってたんだよ！
English: You're late, Edgey! What took you so long!?

Japanese: ‥‥ムリを言うな。ジェット機を チャーターして飛んできたのだ。
English: I don't want to hear it! I chartered a private jet to come as quickly as I could!

Japanese: どうせチャーターするんなら、 もっと速いヤツにしろっての。
English: Well, you should've chartered a faster one!

Japanese: とにかく、聞いてくれよ！
English: Anyway, just listen!

Japanese: エリス先生がナニで、成歩堂が 真宵ちゃんとあやめちゃんのビキニ
English: Something happened to Ms. Elise... and Nick is... Maya... and Iris's Bikini...
...Huh?

Japanese: ちゃんとしゃべれよ！　御剣ィ！
English: Say something, Edgey!

Japanese: ‥‥こちらへ来る前、成歩堂が 入院している病院に行ってきた。
English: ...Before I came here, I stopped in at the hospital where Wright is.

Japanese: 状況は‥‥少なくとも、 キミよりはキチンとわかっている。
English: I believe I have a better understanding of the situation than you, at this point.

Japanese: ‥‥殺害されたのは、 絵本作家の天流斎　エリス。
English: The murder victim was the picture book author, Ms. Elise Deauxnim.

Japanese: 発見者は、住職と成歩堂。 容疑者は‥‥葉桜院の尼僧。
English: She was found by Wright and the head nun. The suspect is the temple's younger nun.

Japanese: そして、成歩堂は‥‥吊り橋を 踏みコワして、急流に落下。
English: Then later, while Wright was crossing the bridge, it broke and he fell into the river.

Japanese: とりあえず、２日間は病院で 絶対安静‥‥ということらしい。
English: The hospital says that he'll need at least 2 days of bed rest.

Japanese: そ、そう！　つまり‥‥ そういうコトなワケよ！
English: Y-Yes! Th-That's right! You got it!

Japanese: オレのあやめちゃんが‥‥ つかまっちまったんだよおお！
English: B-But they arrested her... My sweet little Iris!

Japanese: （‥‥てっきり、コイツが 逮捕されたものと思っていた）
English: (And here I was, convinced HE was the one the police had arrested...)

Japanese: ただ‥‥、よくわからないのは この２つのシロモノだ。
English: However... I still don't understand what these two items are for.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだよ。
English: What are you talking about?

Japanese: 病室を出るとき、 成歩堂にたくされたのだが‥‥
English: They're things Wright gave to me when I was leaving his room.

Japanese: （まず、１つめ‥‥）
English: (This is the first...)

Japanese: （人のココロを見抜く宝玉、とか。 ‥‥とてもアヤシイ‥‥）
English: (He said some nonsense about being able to see into people's hearts with this...)

Japanese: （そして‥‥もう１つ。 ‥‥どういうことだ、これは？）
English: (And the other... He couldn't possibly be asking what I think he is... could he?)

Japanese: たのむよ！　あやめちゃんの裁判 ‥‥もう、明日なんだ！
English: I'm begging you! Iris's trial starts tomorrow!

Japanese: 成歩堂が死んだ今。 もう、オマエしかいねえんだよ！
English: With Nick out of the picture, you're all I've got left!

Japanese: あやめちゃんのベンゴを まかせられるオトコは！
English: You're the only one that can represent her!
What did you just say?

Japanese: ベンゴだよ、ベ・ン・ゴ！ ‥‥決まってるだろ！
English: You know! REPRESENT! DEFEND! What were you expecting!?

Japanese: なんでワザワザ、オマエを 呼びつけたと思ってるんだよ！
English: Why do you think I called you anyway!?

Japanese: ‥‥いいか、矢張。私は検事だぞ。
English: ...I'm a prosecutor, Larry. A prosecutor.

Japanese: わかるか？　“けんじさん” というのは、だ‥‥
English: Do YOU understand what I'm saying? A prosecutor is a lawyer who...

Japanese: バカにするな！ オレだって、中学は出てるんだ！
English: Don't talk to me like a kid! I graduated from junior high, you know!

Japanese: 大丈夫だって！ バレやしねえからさ！
English: Don't worry about it! I promise I won't tell!

Japanese: い、いや、しかし‥‥
English: But I...

Japanese: ボール紙のニセバッジでも バレなかったってウワサだぜ！
English: I mean, I heard a paper badge had no problem fooling an entire court before!

Japanese: （‥‥それでいいのか‥‥ この国の司法制度はッ！）
English: (...How could this country's judicial system have fallen into such decay!?)

Japanese: とにかく、たのむよ御剣！
English: Please, Edgey!

Japanese: あやめちゃんのハナシ‥‥ 聞くだけでもいいからよお！
English: At least listen to her... Listen to Iris's side of the story!

Japanese: （やはり‥‥冗談ではなかった ようだな。このバッジ‥‥）
English: (So Wright wasn't joking when he gave me this badge after all...)

Japanese: お待たせしました。 ‥‥あやめと申します。
English: Thank you for coming. ...My name is Iris.

Japanese: ‥‥御剣　怜侍です。
English: ...Edgeworth. Miles Edgeworth.

Japanese: おチカラになれるかは わかりませんが‥‥
English: I don't know if I can be of any help, but...

Japanese: お話を、お聞かせねがいましょう。 ‥‥事件について。
English: ...I will at least hear what you have to say. ...About the murder.

Japanese: あ、あの‥‥‥
English: Um...

Japanese: な、成歩堂さんは！ ‥‥ごブジなのでしょうか？
English: M-Mr. Wright! H-H-How is he?

Japanese: マシスさまのお話では、 お、お亡くなりになった、とか‥‥
English: Mr. Laurice said that he... That he might even die...

Japanese: さいわい、生きています。 （矢張め。テキトーなコトを‥‥）
English: Fortunately, he will be fine. (Larry, you moron! How could you say something like that!?)

Japanese: 水面に落ちた際に全身を強く 打ったようだが、ケガはない。
English: He was badly bruised when he hit the water, but otherwise he is unharmed.

Japanese: ‥‥よかった‥‥
English: Thank goodness...

Japanese: ただし。‥‥タチの悪い カゼをひいたようです。
English: But, he's caught some kind of nasty cold.

Japanese: ‥‥おカゼを‥‥？
English: A cold...?

Japanese: 超高熱にうなされ、モーローと していました。
English: He's running a high fever and is drifting in and out of consciousness.

Japanese: （気のせい、だろうか‥‥）
English: (I must be imagining things.)

Japanese: （この女性。‥‥どこかで、 見たことがあるような‥‥）
English: (This woman... I feel like I've met her before...)

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら‥‥
English: Hmm...

Japanese: あなたから聞ける 情報は、これぐらいのようだ。
English: It appears that's about all that you can tell me.

Japanese: ありがとうございました。 ‥‥話を聞いていただいて。
English: Thank you very much for listening to my story.

Japanese: ‥‥今日、ここへ来る前、 私は成歩堂の病院をたずねました。
English: ...I visited Wright at the hospital before coming here.

Japanese: そこで、彼からたのまれたのです。 ‥‥あなたのことを。
English: He asked me to take care of you...

Japanese: 私‥‥ですか？
明日の裁判‥‥ あなたを守ってほしい、と。
English: Yes, at the trial tomorrow... He asked me to defend you.

Japanese: 成歩堂さんが そうおっしゃったのならば‥‥
English: If Mr. Wright has that much faith in you, Mr. Edgeworth...

Japanese: ‥‥御剣さま。 あなたに、おまかせします。
English: ...Then I will gladly entrust my fate in your capable hands.

Japanese: その前に、１つだけ。 うかがっておきたい。
English: But before that, I have one question.

Japanese: なんでしょうか‥‥？
English: Yes?

Japanese: あなたは、成歩堂を ご存じなのですね？
English: Do you know Wright?

Japanese: え‥‥。 ど、どうしてですか？
English: Er... Wh-Why would you ask that?

Japanese: どうも、あなたのことを話す 彼のタイドが、妙だった。
English: Whenever you came up in our conversation, he would begin to act a little... strange.

Japanese: 御剣さまは‥‥、あの方の お友だち、なのですか？
English: Mr. Edgeworth... Are you his friend?

Japanese: ‥‥かけがえのない友人です。
English: Friend? Well... In a sense, yes.

Japanese: 今から、５年ほど前‥‥
English: It was 5 years ago...

Japanese: 私は、あの方を あざむいたのです。
English: That's when I... That's when I... deceived him.

Japanese: “あざむいた”‥‥？
English: You "deceived" him...?

Japanese: あの方は‥‥ずいぶん、 お苦しみになったと聞いています。
English: I heard that he was... in a lot of pain after what happened.

Japanese: 私が、弱い人間だったから‥‥ いけなかったのです。
English: I know what a weak person I am.

Japanese: だから‥‥二度と姿を見せまい、 と思っていました。
English: That's why... That's why I thought it was best if he never saw me again.

Japanese: 何も知らないまま、すべてを 忘れてほしかったのです。
English: I wanted him to just forget about me, without learning the truth.

Japanese: 成歩堂は、今もなお、 苦しんでいるようでした。
English: Well, if you ask me, Wright is still suffering...

Japanese: 真実を知らないかぎり、 彼は永久に苦しむでしょう。
English: And until he learns the truth, I don't think he will ever be able to truly recover.

Japanese: ‥‥あやめさん。 今からでも、おそくはない。
English: ...Iris. It's not too late.

Japanese: 彼にだけは‥‥真実を 話してやってください。
English: You should go to him. ...Tell him the truth.

Japanese: それが‥‥あなたの 弁護を引き受ける、条件です。
English: I'll defend you, but only if you agree to that one condition.

Japanese: わかりました。 ‥‥お約束します、御剣さま。
English: Alright, Mr. Edgeworth... I promise.

Japanese: ‥‥了解しました。 全力をつくしましょう。
English: ...Very well. I'll do everything in my power to get you an acquittal.

Japanese: （“情報”はそろった。 そろそろ、現場へ向かうか‥‥）
English: (That's enough information gathering for now. I should head to the crime scene...)

Japanese: ‥‥事件現場は、野獣のひそむ ジャングルと同じ。
English: ...Going to a crime scene is akin to entering a jungle teeming with dangerous beasts.

Japanese: そこへおもむく狩人は、まず、 弾薬の補給をしなければならない。
English: Before he goes there, a hunter needs to make sure he has plenty of ammunition.

Japanese: “情報”という名の弾薬を。 ‥‥すべては、そこからだ。
English: And in my case, that ammunition is called, "information".

Japanese: 守衛が、こちらを ニラみつけている。
English: The guard is glaring at me.

Japanese: “ニラまれたら、ニラみ返せ” ‥‥私は、そう教えられてきた。
English: "If someone glares at you, it's only polite to return the favor," is what I was taught.

Japanese: あ、あの。 大丈夫ですか？　御剣さま。
English: Um... Are you alright, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: ‥‥む。し、シツレイ。
English: ...Hm? Oh, e-excuse me.

Japanese: カメラが、こちらを ニラみつけている。
English: The camera is glaring at me.

Japanese: “ニラまれたら、ニラみ返せ” ‥‥私は、そう教えられてきた。
English: "If something glares at you, it's only polite to return the favor," is what I was taught.

Japanese: あ、あの。 どこか、具合でも‥‥？
English: Um... Is something bothering you?

Japanese: ‥‥む。し、シツレイ。
English: ...Hm? Oh, e-excuse me.

Japanese: シツレイですが‥‥ あやめさん、でしたね。
English: Pardon me... Iris. I would like to ask you something, if you don't mind.

Japanese: 以前どこかで、お会いしたような 気がするのだが‥‥。
English: I have the distinct feeling you and I have met before...

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥ 気のせいだと思います。
English: I-It must be your imagination, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: 私‥‥葉桜院をはなれることは、 あまりありませんから。
English: After all... I hardly ever leave Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: 《葉桜院》というのは？
English: Hazakura Temple? What's that?

Japanese: 霊行道をこころざす者たちが 集う、宿坊です。
English: It's a place where those who wish to boost their spiritual power come to train.

Japanese: 霊力を解放するためには、きびしい 修行を重ねる必要があるのです。
English: You need to undergo some very difficult training to release your inner spiritual power.

Japanese: 霊力‥‥。 あなたも、そのための修行を‥‥？
English: Spiritual power...? Did you go to that temple for that reason as well?

Japanese: いいえ。‥‥私には、 霊力など、ございません。
English: No... I don't have any spiritual powers.

Japanese: そんなもの‥‥ 必要ないですから。
English: I don't need them.

Japanese: それならば‥‥なぜ、 その宿坊におられるのか？
English: In that case, what are you doing at that temple then?

Japanese: 私には‥‥つぐなわなければ ならない《罪》があるのです。
English: I... I've committed some sins. Sins that I need to pay for.

Japanese: だから‥‥修行をつづけています。
English: That's why I'm there, and why I continue to train... To purify my soul.

Japanese: 事件当夜‥‥ゆうべのことを 聞かせていただきたい。
English: I want to ask you about last night... The night of the crime.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: Alright...

Japanese: ‥‥お夕食のかたづけをすませて、 自室に戻ったのが、８時ごろ。
English: I helped to clean up after dinner, and then went back to my room at about 8:00.

Japanese: そのあと、１０時に 《消灯の鐘》を鳴らしました。 at 10:00.
English: Later, I left my room to ring the lights out bell

Japanese: 鐘を‥‥？
English: Bell...?

Japanese: ‥‥毎晩、同じ時間に 鳴らすことになっているのです。
English: ...We ring it at the same time each night.

Japanese: なるほど。‥‥それから？
English: I see... And then?

Japanese: そ、それから‥‥
English: A-And then...

Japanese: 修験堂に来るように 言われていたのですが‥‥。
English: I was told to go to the Training Hall, but...

Japanese: ずっと‥‥自室におりました。
English: I went back to my room... and stayed there...

Japanese: なぜ‥‥その《修験堂》に 行かなかったのですか？
English: Why didn't you go to the Training Hall like you were asked to?

Japanese: ‥‥こ、こわかったものですから。
English: ...I-I was frightened.

Japanese: （“こわかった”‥‥？）
English: ("Frightened"...?)

Japanese: それで‥‥事件が起こるまで、 自室で瞑想をしておりました。
English: So I just stayed in my room and meditated... until the murder happened.

Japanese: （何か‥‥事情がありそうだ。 もう少し、聞いてみるか‥‥）
English: (There's more to her story. I just know there is. Maybe I should dig a little deeper.)

Japanese: この事件について‥‥ 何か、心当たりはあるだろうか。
English: So do you have any idea as to what really occurred last night?

Japanese: おそらく‥‥すべては、霊力が 引き起こしたのだと思います。
English: ...I think it was the result of the tremendous spiritual power that was unleashed.
Spiritual power...?

Japanese: はい。霊行道は、古くから ヒゲキを引き起こしてきました。
English: Yes. Spiritual training has been a cause behind many great tragedies.

Japanese: ‥‥今回の事件も、また‥‥
English: This incident was just another example...

Japanese: ‥‥あやめさん。 そのコトバには、同意できない。
English: ...Iris, I'm sorry, but I can't accept that.

Japanese: 私は、《霊力》というものの 存在は、信じられそうにない。
English: I'm a man of science. I don't believe in "spiritual power".

Japanese: ええ‥‥。 それがフツウだと思います。
English: Yes... I understand. Most people don't.

Japanese: 被害者を殺害したのは まちがいなく、人間なのです。
English: And I am certain that the thing that killed the victim was a human.

Japanese: ひとつ‥‥カクニンさせて いただこう。
English: So please, answer me this simple question...

Japanese: 天流斎　エリスを殺害したのは ‥‥あなた、なのだろうか？
English: Were you the one who killed Elise Deauxnim?

Japanese: ちがいます。
English: No.

Japanese: あの方の命をうばったのは、 私ではありません。
English: I'm not the one who took her life.
Hmm...

Japanese: （‥‥我ながら、愚かなことを 聞いたものだ）
English: (That was foolish of me...)

Japanese: （真実を述べているかどうかなど、 わかるハズもないのに‥‥）
English: (It's not as if I can know for certain if she is telling me the truth...)

Japanese: （《さいころ錠》が現れない‥‥）
English: (Those Psycholock things aren't appearing...)

Japanese: （“ウソをついていない”と 考えていいわけか‥‥）
English: (I suppose that means I can believe that she's not lying.)

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。
English: ...Heh.

Japanese: どうされましたか？
English: What's wrong?

Japanese: （‥‥我ながら‥‥）
English: (I can't believe what I'm thinking...)

Japanese: （“霊力など信じない”と言った オトコの思考ではないな‥‥）
English: (And here I just finished saying that I don't believe in spiritual power...)

Japanese: 事件当夜、修験堂に来るように 言われていたのですね？
English: You were asked to go to the Training Hall on the night of the murder?

Japanese: はい‥‥。
English: Yes...

Japanese: しかし‥‥ あなたは、行かなかった。
English: However... You didn't go.

Japanese: “こわかったから”という理由で。
English: Because you say you were frightened.

Japanese: いったい‥‥何が “こわかった”のだろうか？
English: What exactly were you so frightened of?

Japanese: （な‥‥なんだ、これは！）
English: (Wh-What in the world!?)

Japanese: あの‥‥。 どうかされましたか？
English: Um... Is there something wrong?

Japanese: い、いや。‥‥シツレイ。 （本人には見えないようだな‥‥）
English: ...I'm sorry. It's nothing. (It looks like she's not aware of them herself...)

Japanese: （これが、成歩堂の言っていた ‥‥《さいころ錠》！）
English: (These must be what Wright was talking about... The "Psycholocks"!)

Japanese: （たしか‥‥この勾玉を “つきつける”と聞いたが‥‥）
English: (I believe he said that I need to present this "Magatama" item to do something...)

Japanese: 私‥‥夜に出歩くのは、 あまり好きではないんです。
English: I... I don't like to walk alone at night.

Japanese: それに、あの晩は‥‥
English: And, last night was...

Japanese: なんだろうか？
English: What about last night?

Japanese: い、いえ‥‥べつに。 つ、つまらないことです。
English: Er, oh... nothing. It's rather silly...

Japanese: やはり‥‥ゆうべは、何か特別な コトがあったようですね。
English: So there was something special about last night, hmm?

Japanese: うううう‥‥。
English: Nngh...

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥この錠を 解除するほか、ないようだな）
English: (It looks like I have no choice but to break these locks...)

Japanese: それが、弁護士さまの バッジですか‥‥
English: Is that an attorney's badge?

Japanese: ‥‥まあ、これは 成歩堂のものなのですが。
English: Yes, this belongs to Wright.

Japanese: そうなのですか‥‥？
English: Really?

Japanese: 私自身は、検事なのです。
English: I'm actually a prosecutor.

Japanese: まあ‥‥！　検事さまがなぜ、 そのようなバッジをエリに‥‥？
English: Oh...! Then why do you have a defense attorney's badge pinned on your lapel...?

Japanese: なんとなく、その。 ‥‥なりゆきで。
English: Well, you see... I, um... I borrowed it.

Japanese: ‥‥私は反対したのです。 雑誌の取材なんて。
English: ...I was against the idea of our temple appearing in that magazine.

Japanese: 恐れていたとおり‥‥ このようなヒゲキが起こりました。
English: I was afraid that... this type of a tragedy might occur.

Japanese: それならば‥‥取材など、 断ればよかったのでは‥‥？
English: In that case, why did you allow them to run an article about you?

Japanese: 住職さまは‥‥意外に 目立ちたがり屋さま、ですの。
English: It was Sister Bikini... She's actually rather fond of attention... Surprisingly so.

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥異常に うれしそうだな‥‥）
English: (She certainly does look rather happy in this photo...)

Japanese: この掛け軸に描かれた女性は‥‥？
English: Who is the woman on this hanging scroll?

Japanese: 詳しくは存じませんが‥‥
English: Well, I don't know the details, but...

Japanese: 倉院流霊媒道の家元さま ‥‥だそうです。
English: I've heard she's the Master of the Kurain Channeling Technique.

Japanese: い、イエモト‥‥？
English: M-Master?

Japanese: 舞子さま。“偉大な霊媒師” ‥‥そう聞いております。
English: Yes. Mystic Misty. She was a great spirit medium. ...That's what I've heard.

Japanese: 消息を絶ってから、もう １５年以上たつ、とか‥‥。
English: I also heard that she went missing over 15 years ago.

Japanese: 霊媒‥‥など‥‥、 できるわけがない。
English: Spirit mediums... There's no such thing anyway...

Japanese: 御剣さま‥‥？
English: Mr. Edgeworth...?

Japanese: 私は知っているのです。 ‥‥彼らがインチキであることを！
English: I know all about them... They're nothing but frauds!

Japanese: （もちろん、彼女は知るまい‥‥）
English: (She doesn't know anything about it, of course...)

Japanese: （１７年前‥‥この私自身が、 “家元”に会っていることなど）
English: (But there was a time, 17 years ago, when I met the "Master" myself...)

Japanese: ‥‥《退魔のずきん》と 呼ばれるものです。
English: That's called a "Demon-Warding Hood".

Japanese: ゆうべ‥‥成歩堂さんに さしあげました。
English: ...I gave that to Mr. Wright last night.

Japanese: 今日、病室をたずねたら、 コイツをかぶっていました。
English: He was wearing this when I saw him at the hospital today.

Japanese: 悪霊から身を守る、修験者にとって とてもダイジなものです。
English: It's an important item for protecting acolytes from evil spirits.

Japanese: そんなものを、なぜ‥‥彼に？
English: If it's so important, then why did you give it to him?

Japanese: ‥‥私‥‥感じていました。 ゆうべ、何かが‥‥
English: Last night... I felt... something...

Japanese: 何か、取りかえしのつかない ことが起こる、って‥‥。
English: I felt that something terrible was about to happen...

Japanese: 成歩堂さんが、それに巻きこまれて ほしくなかったのです。
English: I... I didn't want Mr. Wright to fall into its grip.

Japanese: （これ以上ないほど冷たい急流に 巻きこまれたようだが‥‥）
English: (Well he wound up falling into something much deeper and colder...)

Japanese: （それは言わないほうが いいだろう‥‥）
English: (I probably shouldn't say that out loud, though. Thank god for inner monologue.)

Japanese: 天流斎　エリスさま。 絵本作家‥‥と聞いております。
English: That's Mystic Elise Deauxnim. She's a picture book author, or so I've heard.

Japanese: 彼女は、よく葉桜院へ‥‥？
English: Does she come to Hazakura Temple often...?

Japanese: いいえ‥‥いらっしゃるのは 初めてだと存じます。
English: No... This was her first time.

Japanese: ただ‥‥
English: It's just that...

Japanese: とても大切なお客さま、 だったようです。
English: She was a very important visitor.

Japanese: そうなのですか。
English: Is that so?

Japanese: “くれぐれも失礼のないように” ‥‥と、住職さまに。
English: Yes, Sister Bikini told me, "Be certain not to offend her."

Japanese: （被害者の、天流斎　エリス）
English: (The victim, Ms. Elise Deauxnim...)

Japanese: （検事局でも、まだ情報が 集まっていないらしい‥‥）
English: (The prosecutor's office still doesn't have much information about her it seems...)

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂さん‥‥ おカゼのぐあいは、いかがですか？
English: Mr. Wright... How bad is his cold?

Japanese: とにかく、熱がヒドい。 とてもコンランしています。
English: Well his fever is very high. As a result, he's rather confused.

Japanese: 殺人事件に、奥の院に 閉じこめられた、真宵くん‥‥
English: He's worried about Maya, who is still trapped in the Inner Temple...

Japanese: そして、あなたのコトで、 かなり苦しんでいるようでした。
English: ...And he's quite worried about you as well, it seems.

Japanese: ‥‥そうですか‥‥
English: ...Really?

Japanese: まあ‥‥彼が回復したら、 バトンをわたすつもりです。
English: Yes... Naturally, once he recovers, I'll pass the baton back to him.

Japanese: いいえ‥‥
English: No... Don't...

Japanese: きっと、成歩堂さんは、 引き受けてくださらないでしょう。
English: I'm sure that Mr. Wright wouldn't want that...

Japanese: こんな、私の弁護など‥‥
English: He wouldn't want to defend me.

Japanese: ‥‥葉桜院の住職、と うかがっているが。
English: ...I'd like to ask you about Sister Bikini, the head of Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: ええ。‥‥私を、母親のように 育ててくださいました。
English: She raised me as if she were my real mother.

Japanese: “母親のように”‥‥？
English: "As if she were your real mother"...?

Japanese: 私‥‥おさないころ、 この霊行道場にあずけられました。
English: I was left at Hazakura Temple when I was just a small child.

Japanese: それ以来ずっと、 お世話になっております。
English: And she's taken care of me ever since.

Japanese: なぜ‥‥？　失礼ですが、 ご家族は‥‥
English: Forgive me for sounding rude, but what about your family?

Japanese: いろいろ、事情がありまして。
English: I'm sorry, but I'd rather not talk about this.

Japanese: ‥‥私のことなど、 聞く価値はありません。
English: You want to know about me? There's not much to tell, I'm afraid.

Japanese: あなたは、葉桜院で 育ったそうですね‥‥。
English: Just let me get one thing straight. You were raised at Hazakura Temple, correct?

Japanese: それなのに。成歩堂は あなたを知っているようだった。
English: And yet, somehow, you seem to know Wright.

Japanese: つまり‥‥どこかに、 接点があったはずなのです。
English: So you two must have crossed paths somewhere.

Japanese: ‥‥いいえ。 きっと、それは‥‥
English: ...No, Mr. Edgeworth. Whomever it was...

Japanese: 私ではないと思います。
English: ...it couldn't have been me.

Japanese: （‥‥どういうことだ‥‥？）
English: (What is that supposed to mean?)

Japanese: あ‥‥マシスさま、ですね。
English: Oh... That's Mr. Laurice.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥申しわけない。 どなた、ですって？
English: ...I'm sorry? Who did you say it was?

Japanese: 天流斎　マシスさま。 エリスさまのお弟子、でしょう？
English: Laurice Deauxnim. He is Mystic Elise's apprentice, I think.

Japanese: （だ、ダレだ、そのオトコ‥‥）
English: (Alright... Now who exactly is this guy...?)

Japanese: とてもマジメで、 誠実そうなかたですよね。
English: He's a very sincere, hard-working person.

Japanese: あの‥‥。 どうかされましたか？　御剣さま。
English: Um... Did I say something wrong, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: し、シツレイ。 コトバを失ってしまいました。
English: E-Excuse me. I was temporarily at a loss for words.

Japanese: （‥‥私の知らないところで、 ナニが起こっているのか‥‥？）
English: (Take a trip... Come home... Be thrust suddenly into bizarro world...)

Japanese: ‥‥申しわけありません。
English: I'm sorry...

Japanese: 私、葉桜院から あまり出ませんので。
English: I don't leave the temple grounds very often...

Japanese: 世間のこと、よく存じませんの。
English: So I don't know very much about the outside world.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: February 8 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥あやめさんは 取り調べ中のようだ）
English: (It looks like Iris is being interrogated right now.)

Japanese: それにしても‥‥ なつかしいな、ここは。
English: ...This place certainly brings back memories.

Japanese: そういえば、御剣検事も ブチ込まれたッスねー、ここに。
English: Oh yeah. Come to think of it, you got thrown in here once too, didn't you, sir?

Japanese: ちなみに、自分は 入ったコト、ないッス！
English: But you know what? I've never been in jail a single time!

Japanese: （アタリマエだろう‥‥）
English: (I should think it's hard to land in jail when you're so harmless...)

Japanese: だが‥‥
English: Yes, well...

Japanese: ここに入ると、食事代は かからないが、な。
English: If you're in jail, you don't have to pay for your own meals, you know.

Japanese: ‥‥ッス！
English: ...*gasp!*

Japanese: よからぬコトは考えぬ コトだ、刑事‥‥。
English: Don't get any funny ideas, Detective...!

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっぴり、 考えちまったッス。
English: ...Too late, I already did.

Japanese: （とにかく‥‥また あとで来てみるか‥‥）
English: (In any case... I guess I'll have to come back here later...)

Japanese: 守衛が、私のことを フシギそうに見ている。
English: The guard is staring at me with a puzzled expression on his face.

Japanese: 私と‥‥エリのバッジを。
English: Me... and the badge on my lapel.

Japanese: どうやら、私のことを 知っているらしいな‥‥
English: ...I get the feeling he recognizes me.

Japanese: 面会のようすを 監視するカメラだ。
English: That camera is meant to monitor everything that happens in here.

Japanese: 今は、守衛を監視しているようだ。
English: And right now, it's pointed straight at the guard.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 留置所　面会室
English: February 8 Detention Center Visitor's Room

Japanese: あ‥‥御剣さま‥‥。
English: Ah... Mr. Edgeworth...

Japanese: ‥‥もう一度、お話をうかがおうと 思って、おじゃましました。
English: ...I came back because I need to ask you a few more questions, if you don't mind.

Japanese: でも‥‥私。 お話しできることは、みんな‥‥
English: But I... I've already told you everything that I...

Japanese: ‥‥あやめさん。 忘れないでいただきたい。
English: ...Iris. Please remember.

Japanese: 私は、あなたのミカタだ。 なんでも話してもらいたいのです。
English: I'm on your side. You can tell me anything.
Y-Yes... Th-Thank you.

Japanese: ‥‥明日の裁判‥‥ 私はかならず、勝ちます。
English: The trial starts tomorrow... I promise you, I will win.

Japanese: あなたを、もう一度 成歩堂　龍一に会わせるために。
English: I'm going to win so that you and Phoenix Wright can see each other again.

Japanese: そのときは‥‥
English: But when I do...

Japanese: あなたの口から、彼に “秘密”を語っていただきたい。
English: ...you must promise me that you will tell him your secret.

Japanese: きっと‥‥ムダ、だと思います。
English: But it's pointless...
Why would you say that?

Japanese: たしかに、私は 成歩堂さんを知っています。
English: Because I may know who Phoenix Wright is...

Japanese: でも‥‥あの方は、私を 知らないでしょうから‥‥
English: But... He has no idea who I am.

Japanese: 先ほど、葉桜院の住職から 話を聞いてきました。
English: I just finished speaking with the head nun of Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: ‥‥彼女は、ハッキリ 証言しています。
English: She testified very clearly as to what happened.

Japanese: あなたが、天流斎　エリスを 刀で刺したところを見た、と。
English: She said she saw you stab Ms. Elise Deauxnim with a sword.

Japanese: そして、もう１つ。
English: And one other thing.

Japanese: 奥の院で、綾里　真宵の 修行を始めるとき‥‥
English: She said that when Maya Fey began her training at the Inner Temple...

Japanese: そこに、あなたがいた‥‥と。
English: ...you were there as well.

Japanese: え‥‥っ！
English: What...!?

Japanese: あなたは『奥の院へは行ってない』 と、おっしゃっている。
English: When I spoke with you last, you claimed that you never went to the Inner Temple.

Japanese: しかし‥‥毘忌尼さんは、奥の院で あなたと会っているのですよ。
English: And yet, Sister Bikini says she met with you at the Inner Temple that very night.

Japanese: わ‥‥私は‥‥
English: B-But I...

Japanese: い、行っておりません！ ゆうべ、奥の院へは‥‥。
English: I didn't go there! I didn't go to the Inner Temple last night.

Japanese: （‥‥どうやら‥‥すんなり 話してはくれないようだ）
English: (Hmm... It looks like she's unwilling to tell me the whole truth.)

Japanese: （《さいころ錠》を解けば、 コタエがわかるのだろうか？）
English: (I wonder if I'll find the answers I'm looking for if I break those Psycholocks?)

Japanese: お夕食のあと‥‥私の部屋に、 このお手紙がありました。
English: After dinner, this letter was waiting for me in my room.

Japanese: ‥‥お察しのとおりです。 私、こわくなってしまって‥‥
English: As I said, I was frightened by it...

Japanese: この、手紙に書かれてる 《極楽庵》というのは？
English: What is this "Heavenly Hall" the letter mentions?

Japanese: 山小屋、のようなものです。 おぼろ橋のたもとにある‥‥
English: It's a small mountain shack at the base of Dusky Bridge.

Japanese: 山小屋‥‥
English: A small shack, huh...?

Japanese: だれも近寄ることのない、 あばら屋です。
English: It's more like a broken-down shack that no one would ever want to go near.

Japanese: むうう‥‥。 この見取り図でいうと‥‥？
English: Hmm... Where is it on this map...?

Japanese: ‥‥このあたりです。 おぼろ橋から、細い道をおりて‥‥
English: ...It's around here. To get there, you must follow a small path down from Dusky Bridge...

Japanese: 奥の院へ行くには、どうしても 橋をわたらなければなりません。
English: The reality is, to get to the Inner Temple, I had no choice but to cross that bridge.

Japanese: この極楽庵に、恐ろしい怪人が 待ちかまえていると思うと‥‥
English: But the thought that such a terrible criminal could be lurking at Heavenly Hall...

Japanese: 私。なんとしても、 思い出したくなかったんです。
English: I... I was so scared by the whole affair that I didn't want to think about it at all.

Japanese: ‥‥あなたのココロに閉ざされた “秘密”‥‥ですか。
English: So is this the secret that you locked away in your heart?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。
English: ...Yes.

Japanese: （《極楽庵》‥‥調べてみる 必要があるようだ）
English: (It looks as though I may have to visit this "Heavenly Hall" now.)

Japanese: （脅迫者の痕跡が 残されているかもしれない‥‥）
English: (Maybe I'll find some sign of our mystery blackmailer.)

Japanese: とにかく、あなたはゆうべ、 自室から出ていないのですね？
English: In any case, you still claim to have never left your room last night?

Japanese: はい。まちがいありません。
English: Yes. That's exactly right.

Japanese: 証拠品《あやめの証言書》を 法廷記録に挟んだ。
English: Iris's Testimony added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ああ‥‥ゆうべの 修験者さまですね。
English: Oh... This is the woman who was to undergo training last night.

Japanese: とても、しっかり者で たよりになりそうな方ですね。
English: She seems like a very strong, reliable woman.

Japanese: （どうも‥‥）
English: (Whatever else anyone has to say about this Iris woman...)

Japanese: （この女性の“ヒトを見る目” には、やや疑問を感じる‥‥）
English: (...I can't exactly say she's the best judge of character.)

Japanese: 修験者さまの 妹さんでしょうか。
English: This is the trainee's younger sister, correct?

Japanese: とてもかわいらしいですね。 カレーが大好きなんですよ。
English: She's cute as a button! And she seems to really love mashed potatoes and gravy!

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
ゆうべも、あまったカレーを お夜食にするんだ、と言って‥‥
English: She even said she was going to have the leftovers for dinner the next night...

Japanese: おナベをお部屋に 持って帰りましたから。
English: And then she took the leftover potatoes and the whole pot of gravy with her to her room.

Japanese: （よく食べるな‥‥ 見かけによらず）
English: (That's quite an appetite for such a little girl...)

Japanese: あ‥‥マシスさま、ですね。
English: Oh... That's Mr. Laurice.

Japanese: とてもマジメで、 誠実そうなかたですよね。
English: He's a very sincere, hard-working person.

Japanese: 私、似顔絵を 描いていただきました。
English: He was even kind enough to draw a portrait of me.

Japanese: （考えてみれば、この女性‥‥）
English: (Knowing Larry...)

Japanese: （あのオトコが、ひとめで 懸想するタイプではあるな）
English: (...this woman is exactly the type that he would fall in love with at first sight.)

Japanese: これは‥‥あなた宛ての 手紙のようですが‥‥？
English: This letter... It appears to be addressed to you.

Japanese: きっと、たちのよくない いたずらだと思います。
English: I think it's someone's idea of a joke, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: だから‥‥捨ててしまいましたの。
English: That's why I threw it away.

Japanese: （ビミョーに 目が泳いでいるな‥‥）
English: (She's not giving me a straight answer for some reason.)

Japanese: （ベツの方向から 情報を集めたほうがよさそうだ）
English: (I'm going to have to find someone else who can give me more information about this.)

Japanese: そ、それが‥‥凶器ですの？
English: I-Is that the murder weapon?

Japanese: そうです。 ‥‥見おぼえはありますか？
English: Yes, it is. Have you ever seen it before?

Japanese: え、ええ。‥‥境内の、供子さまが お持ちになっていた‥‥
English: Y-Yes. Mystic Ami was holding it... in the courtyard...

Japanese: （住職は、目撃しているらしい）
English: (And apparently, the head nun witnessed the whole sad affair...)

Japanese: （ゆうべ、あなたがこの刀で 天流斎　エリスを‥‥）
English: (She saw you stabbing Ms. Elise Deauxnim with this very sword last night...)

Japanese: ～“こわかった”～
English: -- I Was Frightened --

Japanese: 私が事件をあつかう以上‥‥ すべての疑問にはコタエを出す。
English: Since I have been handed this case, it is my duty to dig up all the answers. Understand?

Japanese: は、はい‥‥
English: Y-Yes, sir...

Japanese: ほんの小さなキッカケが、 事件に新しい光を投げかける‥‥
English: The smallest flame can sometimes bathe a case in a whole new light...

Japanese: 法廷で私は、そんな 瞬間を何度も見てきました。
English: In my years in court, I've seen it happen over and over again.

Japanese: だから、なんとしても探り出す。
English: That's why I'm committed to searching until I have those answers.

Japanese: あなたはゆうべ、本当に 奥の院には行かなかったのか‥‥？
English: Now then, is it really true that you didn't go to the Inner Temple last night...?

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥ 先ほども申し上げました。
English: Y-Yes, I swear... I already told you that.

Japanese: ‥‥そう。あなたはこう言った。 “こわかったから行かなかった”
English: ...Yes. You said you didn't go because you were frightened.

Japanese: ‥‥ええ‥‥そのとおりです。
English: ...Th-That's right.

Japanese: もし、それが本当ならば‥‥
English: If that's the case...

Japanese: 《あなたが何を恐れていたか？》 ‥‥それが問題になります。
English: ...then the obvious question is, "What were you so afraid of?"

Japanese: あやめさん。もしかして‥‥ これではないだろうか。
English: Iris... I wonder.

Japanese: あなたが、外にも出られないほど “こわかった”ものとは‥‥！
English: Is this what frightened you so much that you couldn't even leave your own room!?

Japanese: あなたは、これを恐れていた！ ‥‥いかがかッ！
English: Perhaps THIS is what you were afraid of! ...Well!?

Japanese: ち‥‥、ちがいます‥‥
English: I-I'm sorry, but... you're wrong...

Japanese: フッ‥‥。 声がフルえているようだが‥‥？
English: Huh...? Why is your voice trembling like that?

Japanese: その‥‥。御剣さまの目が、 お、おそろしくて‥‥
English: It's just that... Your eyes... Your eyes are scaring me...

Japanese: うむ‥‥ッ！ （しまった‥‥リキみすぎたか！）
English: Urk! (Blast! I must be trying too hard to bluff.)

Japanese: ‥‥シツレイ。 まだ、なれていないもので。
English: ...Please excuse me. I'm still not used to this role I've been assigned.

Japanese: い、いえ。私のほうこそ、 こわがりで‥‥すみません。
English: N-No, I should apologize... I'm sorry for being such a scaredy-cat.

Japanese: とにかく‥‥ 疑問のコタエは見つけだします。
English: Anyway... I'm still determined to find the answers to this mystery.

Japanese: 私‥‥ゆうべは、本当に 自室におりましたの。
English: But I'm telling you, I really was in my room all last night.

Japanese: こいつは‥‥本堂で見つけました。 あなたに宛てられた手紙です。
English: I found this in the Main Hall. It is addressed to you.

Japanese: あ‥‥そ、それは‥‥！
English: Ah... Th-That's...!

Japanese: ‥‥いかがかな、あやめさん‥‥
English: Well, Iris...?

Japanese: そ‥‥そんなに ニラまないでください‥‥
English: Why... Why are you glaring at me like that?

Japanese: あなたが恐れていたのは、 この手紙の脅迫者だったのです！
English: You were scared of the blackmailer who wrote this to you, isn't that correct!?

Japanese: きゃああっ！
English: Aaaaah!

Japanese: （証拠のチカラというより、私の 眼力で錠が砕けたようだが‥‥）
English: (Was it the evidence or the power of my glare that broke that lock...?)

Japanese: （‥‥まあ、ヨシとしておこう）
English: (Oh well, I don't suppose it matters either way.)

Japanese: で‥‥でも、御剣さま。
English: B-But, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだろうか。
English: ...Yes?

Japanese: 私‥‥そのメモは、 い、いたずらだと思いました。
English: I thought that letter was just someone playing a prank on me...

Japanese: “いたずら”‥‥？
English: A "prank"?

Japanese: だって‥‥
English: Well, yes...

Japanese: 私に、たとえ“秘密”が あったとしても‥‥
English: After all, even if I did have a "secret"...

Japanese: 知られてこまるような ヒトは、いませんもの。
English: ...there's no one to tell it to that would cause me any grief.

Japanese: それは、いかがな ものだろうか‥‥。
English: Hmm... I wonder about that.

Japanese: 毘忌尼さまは、私の おかあさまのようなかたです。
English: Sister Bikini is like a mother to me.

Japanese: かくしごとなど、いたしませんわ！
English: I would never hide anything from her!

Japanese: ‥‥もしかしたら、 そうかもしれない。
English: No, you may not have anything to hide under normal circumstances.

Japanese: しかし。ゆうべにかぎっては、 そうではなかった。
English: However! Last night was different.

Japanese: それが、どんなものなのか‥‥ ザンネンながら、私は知らない。
English: Unfortunately, I don't know the exact nature of your secret yet.

Japanese: しかし‥‥
English: However...! Whatever it is...

Japanese: あなたは、その“秘密”を、 この人物には知られたくなかった！
English: ...there is one person you didn't want your secret told to!

Japanese: いかがかな？　あやめさん！
English: Well, Iris?

Japanese: ‥‥御剣さま。 もしかして、あなたも‥‥
English: ...Mr. Edgeworth. Is it just possible that maybe...

Japanese: ココロの深いところに、“秘密”を 抱えていたことがあるのでは‥‥？
English: ...you yourself have a deep, dark secret in your heart?
Why do you say that...!?

Japanese: 自分がそうだから‥‥ わかるのです。
English: As they say, it takes one to know one.

Japanese: （私としたことが‥‥ ココロを見すかされるとは！）
English: (How could she have known about that...? Is she peering into my soul!?)

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、私のココロには、 深い闇がありました。
English: ...It's true that there is a deep-seated darkness in my heart.

Japanese: しかし‥‥その闇を払うには、 それを解放するしかないのです。
English: However, the only way I can get rid of it is to fully uncover the truth!

Japanese: 私の“秘密”‥‥ですか。
English: You mean... the truth behind my secret?

Japanese: 成歩堂　龍一‥‥
English: Phoenix Wright...

Japanese: あ‥‥っ！
彼にとって、あなたが そうであるように‥‥
English: You mean something to Wright, it seems...

Japanese: あなたにとっても、彼は 特別なイミを持っているようだ。
English: And I can tell he holds a special place in your heart as well.

Japanese: あなたのココロの闇‥‥“秘密”。 彼にだけは、知られたくなかった。
English: That's why you didn't want him, of all people, to know your deep, dark secret.

Japanese: いかがだろうか‥‥？
English: Well? What do you have to say?

Japanese: さすがは‥‥あの方の お友だち、ですね‥‥。
English: I should've expected as much... Especially from a friend of his...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 葉桜院　山門
English: February 8 Hazakura Temple Main Gate

Japanese: おせェぞ御剣！ ナニやってたんだよ！
English: Yo! Edgey! What took you so long!?

Japanese: サムくて、ノーミソが シャーベットになっちまった！
English: I'm so cold, my brain's turned to sherbet.

Japanese: ‥‥急いで帰国したのは、 まちがいだったようだ。
English: ...I knew it was a mistake to race back to this country.

Japanese: な、なんだと‥‥？
English: Wh-What do you mean...?

Japanese: 成歩堂はブジだし、 事件はありふれているし‥‥
English: Wright is going to be fine, and the case itself isn't anything unusual...

Japanese: あろうコトか‥‥その被告人から、 弁護をたのまれるとは！
English: And I find myself taking a request to defend a woman accused of murder!

Japanese: ‥‥ちょ、ちょっと！ 待った！　異議あり！
English: ...H-Hey! Wait a sec! Hold it! Objection!

Japanese: どういうコトッスか、御剣検事！
English: What's going on here, Mr. Edgeworth!?

Japanese: う‥‥うム。セツメイしにくいが、 なりゆきで、そういうコトに‥‥
English: Um, it's hard to explain, but one thing led to another and...

Japanese: なんだよ、その言い方！ それでもオマエ、弁護士かよ！
English: What kind of lame excuse is that!? And you call yourself a defense attorney!?

Japanese: 御剣検事は、検事だからこそ、 御剣検事ッス！
English: Prosecutor Edgeworth is a prosecutor, and that's why he's Prosecutor Edgeworth!

Japanese: “御剣検事弁護士”なんて、 アキラカに、ちょっとヘンッス！
English: "Prosecutor Edgeworth, Defense Attorney" just sounds plain old weird, pal!

Japanese: ‥‥ねー、御剣検事！
English: ...Right, Prosecutor Edgeworth!?

Japanese: （‥‥自分がナニモノか、 よくわからなくなってきた）
English: (...I'm not sure what role I'm supposed to be playing anymore.)

Japanese: 御剣ィッ！ ‥‥しばらく見ないウチに‥‥
English: Hmph! Dude, Edgey... I don't see you for a couple of years...

Japanese: ココロがシャーベットに なっちまったみたいだな！
English: ...and your heart turns to sherbet!

Japanese: ‥‥サムいからな、ここは。
English: ...I'd say more like sorbet. It is rather cold here.

Japanese: あやめちゃんじゃねえよ！ ハンニンは、他にいるんだ！
English: Iris didn't murder her! Someone else did it!

Japanese: オレには‥‥ オレには、わかるんだよお！
English: I just know it, OK!? So trust me on this one!

Japanese: ‥‥あいかわらずの ロマンチストだな、矢張。
English: Ever the romantic, aren't you, Larry?

Japanese: とはいえ‥‥私も引き受けた以上、 全力をつくすしか、あるまい。
English: Nevertheless, I'll do whatever I can to prove her innocence.

Japanese: （‥‥アイツに、 バトンをわたすまでは‥‥）
English: (At least until I pass the baton on to Wright, that is.)

Japanese: かなり古い山門のようだな。 時の重さを支える、重厚な作りだ。
English: This gate looks quite old; strong enough to resist the weight of time.

Japanese: さすが、御剣検事ッス！ コメントがヒトアジちがうッス！
English: That's the Mr. Edgeworth I know! You got a real flair for words, sir!

Japanese: こういう、枯れたフンイキは キライではない。
English: I have always appreciated this kind of old, timeless elegance.

Japanese: やっぱり、古いものには 味があるッスからねえ！
English: That's exactly how I feel, too!

Japanese: 自分のコートも、なんともいえない 色合いで、かつお風味ッス。
English: Take this coat for example! Like a fine wine, it gets better with age!

Japanese: そういう、枯れたフンイキは スキではない。
English: Even fine wine turns to vinegar and begins to stink at some point.

Japanese: ‥‥たまには、洗いたまえ。
English: ...I suggest you wash that atrocious rag you call a coat, Detective.

Japanese: ‥‥こんな山の中では 必要なのかもしれないな。
English: I suppose this type of thing is necessary up here in the mountains.

Japanese: ジツは、これを見ていて ヒラめいたことがあるッス。
English: Say, this just gave me a great idea!

Japanese: （聞きたくないと言っても、 しゃべるんだろうな‥‥）
English: (Something tells me he's going to tell me what's on his mind, whether I like it or not...)

Japanese: まったく新しい発明品ッス！ 大もうけできるかもしれないッス！
English: I've got an idea for a brand new invention! It might even make me rich!

Japanese: 発明品‥‥？
English: An invention...?

Japanese: 雪の上を走るクルマ。その名も 《スノージドーシャ》ッス！
English: Yeah! A car that can travel on snow! I'll call it a "Snow Car"!

Japanese: どッス？　御剣検事も、 ひとくち乗るッスか？
English: So what do you think, sir? Would you go for a ride on something like that?

Japanese: ‥‥試作品ができたら 乗せてもらうとしよう。
English: ...Only after you take the first 1000 test rides.

Japanese: 小さな鐘つき堂が見える。
English: I can see a small bell tower from here.

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば、ムカシから 疑問に思っていたことがあるッス！
English: Hey, that just reminded me! There's something I've always wondered about...

Japanese: （聞きたくないと言っても、 しゃべるんだろうな‥‥）
English: (Something tells me he's going to tell me what's on his mind, whether I like it or not...)

Japanese: ウソをつくヒトを、ワレワレは 《ウソつき》と呼ぶッス。
English: We call a person who tells a lie a "liar", right?

Japanese: ならば！　鐘をつくヒトは やっぱり《カネつき》ッスか？
English: So why don't we call a person who rings a bell a "beller"?

Japanese: 《モチつき》に《マリつき》は！ ‥‥もお、今夜も眠れないッス！
English: Or "truer" for a guy who tells the truth! Oh man, I'm not gonna get any sleep tonight!

Japanese: （意外にナイーブな男だ）
English: (I had no idea he was such a deep thinker...)

Japanese: おそらく‥‥ あそこが本堂だな。
English: That must be the Main Hall back there.

Japanese: ミゴトなスイリッス！
English: A brilliant deduction, sir!

Japanese: それで、おだてたつもりか。 イトノコギリ刑事‥‥
English: Are you trying to flatter me, Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: うううう‥‥ み、ミゴトなサスペンスッス！
English: Umm... A brilliant use of suspense to build the tension, sir!

Japanese: （成歩堂によると、住職の話は かなり重要らしい）
English: (According to Wright, the head nun has some important information.)

Japanese: （聞き逃さないように しないとな‥‥）
English: (I can't miss the opportunity to speak with her...)

Japanese: いやー、それにしても。 カワイイよなあ、あやめちゃん。
English: Man, I'm telling you. Iris is so cute!

Japanese: なー、御剣。オマエも そう思ったろ？　な？
English: Right, Edgey? You think so too, don't you?

Japanese: なんだよ、御剣。 ‥‥ダマりこんじまって。
English: What's wrong? Why are you so quiet?

Japanese: 今のヒトコト‥‥ ヒジョーに気になるのだが。
English: To put it simply... Your comment has me highly concerned.

Japanese: もしかして、おまえが彼女を 無実だと信じている理由は‥‥
English: Could it be that the reason you think she's innocent...

Japanese: あんなカワイイ子が、殺人なんて するワケないからさ！
English: Come on! A girl that cute can't possibly be a murderer!

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり、 帰ってくるんじゃなかった‥‥
English: ...I was right after all. I should never have come back.

Japanese: 大丈夫だって！ オレの目に狂いはねえから！
English: No, no! Don't worry! I see things for how they really are this time! Honest!

Japanese: （何回目だ？　そのセリフ‥‥）
English: (If I had a penny for every time he's said that...)

Japanese: でもなあ‥‥。あやめちゃん、 女の子なんだから。
English: It's just that... Well, Iris is a delicate flower.

Japanese: あんまり、 ムリしちゃいけねえよなあ‥‥
English: You can't force things too much. Know what I mean?

Japanese: ‥‥‥？ 何か言ったか、矢張。
English: Huh...? I have no idea what you're talking about, Larry.

Japanese: ん。‥‥あ！　いやいや。 なんでもねえよ。
English: Oh. ...Ah! Err. Forget it. I didn't say anything...

Japanese: 矢張。事件があった夜は‥‥ どこで、何をしていた？
English: Larry, where were you and what were you doing on the night of the crime?

Japanese: 矢張‥‥？
English: Larry...?

Japanese: ‥‥おッ！　オマエ‥‥ よりによって‥‥
English: What...!? Don't tell me...

Japanese: このオレを！ うた、疑ってるのかよ！
English: You think I might have done it!?
Wh-What...?

Japanese: 帰れ！　もお、ヒコーキ乗って、 どこへでも飛んでっちまえ！
English: Get lost! Go back on your chartered jet and get out of my sight, you creep!

Japanese: ‥‥そして、その途中で ツイラクして死ね！
English: ...And I hope your plane crashes and you die!

Japanese: もう一度だけ、聞く。
English: I'll ask just one more time.

Japanese: 事件があった夜、おまえは どこで、何をしていた？
English: On the night of the murder, where were you and what were you doing?

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥ 《さいころ錠》か！）
English: (As I suspected... a Psycholock!)

Japanese: いやー、さすがのオレも よくおぼえてねえや！
English: I'm sorry, man! You know me, I just don't remember!

Japanese: ‥‥そんなムカシのコト！
English: My short-term memory is a wreck, dude!

Japanese: あやめさんを助けるためには、 情報が必要なのだ。
English: I need all the information I can get if Iris is to have any hope of being acquitted.

Japanese: で、でも！　オレのコトは カンケイねえだろ！
English: B-But I've got nothing to do with any of this!

Japanese: ２５才にもなって定職もなく、 ブラブラしてる絵描きくずれだぜ！
English: I'm just a 25 year old jobless bum trying to be an apprentice artist!

Japanese: （‥‥そう思うのならば シゴトを探せ！）
English: (If that's what you think, then get a job already!)

Japanese: そ、そんな目で見るなよ。 トモダチじゃねえか！
English: D-Don't look at me like that! I thought we were pals!

Japanese: ‥‥トモダチなら、かくしゴトを しないでもらいたいな。
English: If we're friends then I'd appreciate it if you wouldn't hide the truth from me!

Japanese: ま。それはホレ。なんだな。 アレよ、アレ。‥‥わかるだろ？
English: Hmph, well... Sure... It's like, you know! See what I'm saying?

Japanese: （‥‥わからん‥‥）
English: (...I have no idea what you're blathering about.)

Japanese: ‥‥そういえばオマエ、 ムカシ、言ってたっけなァ。
English: Come to think of it, when you were a kid, you were always saying,

Japanese: 『大きくなったら、ボクは弁護士に なるのだよ』‥‥とかなんとか。
English: "When I grow up, I want to be a lawyer and defend people," or something like that.

Japanese: む‥‥ムカシの話だ。
English: ...That was a long time ago.

Japanese: いいじゃねえか。１日だけでも 弁護士になれるんだからさ。
English: But see, now you've got the chance to follow your boyhood dream for a day!

Japanese: 御剣！　オレのあやめちゃん、 守ってやってくれよな。
English: Edgey! You've gotta do it! You gotta save my little Iris!

Japanese: （こ、これだから‥‥古い友人は ヤッカイなのだ‥‥）
English: (Grr... This is exactly why I hate childhood friends...)

Japanese: あややああああああッ！ それ！　あやめちゃんの、ずきん！
English: Aaaaaaaaaaah! Th-That's Iris's hood!

Japanese: 御剣ィィ！　‥‥オマエ‥‥、 あの子のなんなのさ！
English: Why you! Edgey! How could you go behind my back like that!?

Japanese: な、“なんなのさ”と 言われても‥‥
English: Huh? What are you talking about?

Japanese: ズルいぞおおお御剣いいいい！
English: You slimy little weasel!

Japanese: コイツは成歩堂から受け取った。 私のものではないッ！
English: I got this from Wright! He gave it to me!

Japanese: な‥‥、なんだよ。 おどかしやがって。
English: Huh... Oh man... You scared me.

Japanese: でもよぉ。それじゃあ‥‥
English: But... Then that means...

Japanese: 成歩堂は、あの子のなんなのさ？
English: N-Nick went behind my back?

Japanese: 自分で聞けッ！
English: Ask him yourself if you're so worried!

Japanese: いやー、オレ。 こう見えてもゲージュツカだろ？
English: Hey, I know I may not look like it, but I'm an artist.

Japanese: もっとこう、ニオいたつような モチーフを見せてくれねえと。
English: I refuse to look at anything that doesn't have a radiant or beautiful motif.

Japanese: （‥‥見もしない‥‥）
English: (...He didn't even give it a glance.)

Japanese: どうも‥‥彼女は、何かを かくしているようなのだが‥‥
English: I have a feeling that she's hiding something...

Japanese: そうなァ‥‥。彼女、 恥ずかしがり屋さんだからなァ。
English: Could be... After all, she's shy and gets embarrassed pretty easily.

Japanese: ‥‥そういうモンダイでは ないと思うが。
English: ...I don't think that has anything to do with it.

Japanese: テレくさいんだろ。 オレ、よーくリカイできるわ。
English: Why not? Look, I know all about this kind of thing.

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥うん、そうよなァ。 あるよなー、そういうコトって。
English: C'mon Edgey... You were a guy once! Lots of girls are like Iris. You know I'm right!

Japanese: 勝手にリカイして、 勝手にナットクするな！
English: Well, you most certainly seem to have convinced yourself!

Japanese: ‥‥でも、その恥じらいがまた、 たまんないワケよ。わかるだろ？
English: Man, I love shy girls like her! It-It's just so... cute! You know what I mean?

Japanese: （ヒトの話を聞かぬオトコだ ‥‥あいかわらず）
English: (Still doesn't listen to others, I see... I guess some people just never change.)

Japanese: オレなー。どうもそのヒト、 ニガテなんだよなー。
English: I'm just no good with people like her.

Japanese: なんかさ。カアちゃん、 思い出すんだよなあ。
English: She reminds me too much of my mother.

Japanese: いいか、矢張。
English: May I speak now?

Japanese: ナニよ。
English: Huh? Sure.

Japanese: キサマがどう思っているかを 聞いているのではない。
English: I don't care how you feel about her personally...

Japanese: 事件に関して、知ってることを 話してもらいたいな。
English: ...just tell me what you know about her and how she's related to the case.

Japanese: それはオマエ、ちょっと 高望みがすぎるんじゃねえか？
English: Don't you think you're expecting a little too much of me?

Japanese: ２５才にもなって定職もなく、 ブラブラしてる絵描きくずれだぜ！
English: I'm just a 25 year old jobless bum trying to be an apprentice artist!

Japanese: ハッハッハッハッ！
English: Ha ha ha ha!

Japanese: （‥‥ほんの少し、コイツを うらやましく思ってしまった）
English: (...I'm actually starting to feel jealous of this guy. ...Just a little.)

Japanese: ‥‥信じられねえよ。 あんな、いいひとを‥‥
English: ...I still can't believe it. She was such a great person...

Japanese: しかも！　その罪を、 あやめちゃんに着せようなんて！
English: But! Someone's pinning this murder on my sweet little Iris!

Japanese: 御剣！　たのんだぜ！ オレ、応援してるから！
English: Edgey! Please! I'm counting on you!

Japanese: もう少し、マシな 手助けはできないのか？
English: Well... Frankly, I was hoping you could give me a little more than that.

Japanese: ‥‥えー。そうなァ‥‥
English: ...Errr. Well then...

Japanese: じゃあ、アレだ。 オマエの似顔絵、描いてやるわ。
English: I got it! I'll draw you a portrait! How about that!?

Japanese: せっかくだ。‥‥おねがいする。
English: Since you're kind enough to offer... alright.

Japanese: あ。オレのコト、矢張って 呼ぶなよな。
English: Huh? Me? All you have to know is to remember not to call me "Larry" anymore.

Japanese: なんだと‥‥？
English: What...?

Japanese: 今のオレは、身もココロも 天流斎　マシスくんだから。
English: I'm a new person now. I've been reborn... as Laurice Deauxnim!

Japanese: じゃあ、他のハナシを 聞かせてもらおうか、矢張。
English: Alright, fine. Let me ask you something else then, Larry.

Japanese: マシスくん、て呼べぇぇッ！ さもないと、オマエのこと‥‥
English: Call me "Laurice"!! If you don't...

Japanese: 天流斎　ミツルくん、て呼ぶぞお！
English: ...then I'll call you... I'll call you... "Milise Deauxnim"!

Japanese: いいヤツだった‥‥ かもしれねえよな。
English: He was a good guy... A real pal to the end.

Japanese: そう。今になってみりゃ、 みんないい思い出さ。
English: When I look back now, I have nothing but good memories.

Japanese: だから、このオトコは まだ死んでいないッ！
English: What's with the past tense? He's not dead, you know!

Japanese: ま。オレはヤツの イノチの恩人なワケだケドな。
English: Yeah, you're right. Anyway, the guy owes me. After all, I saved his life.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことだ？
English: What is that supposed to mean?

Japanese: オレのすばやい通報で、 成歩堂はブジ、助かったワケよ。
English: If I hadn't called for help so quickly, he would have died.

Japanese: なんと言っても、オレ。 元・ガードマンだし。プロの。
English: It's no surprise though. I used to be a security guard. It was all instinct, baby!

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥私のところにも すぐ、電話がかかってきた‥‥）
English: (He has a point... He called me pretty quickly, too...)

Japanese: （このオトコ、人をつかうのが ウマいのかもしれないな）
English: (I think Larry's pretty good at motivating people.)

Japanese: （自分では、まったく動こうと しないのがハラ立たしいが‥‥）
English: (I just wish he would try motivating himself once in a while!)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 山小屋　極楽庵
English: February 8 Heavenly Hall

Japanese: ひゃー。イタイタしい ながめッスね、またこりゃ。
English: Whoa! Not much of a view down here, huh?

Japanese: いや。自分のアパート‥‥ よりはマシッスかね。
English: It's still better than the view from my apartment, though.

Japanese: むッ！　‥‥だれかいるぞ‥‥！ 身をかくしたまえ、刑事ッ！
English: Tsk! Someone's here! Hide yourself, Detective!

Japanese: あーあ‥‥オレって、 いっつもこうなんだよなー。
English: Oh! Why why why!? Why does this always happen!?

Japanese: 好きなコができると、 かならず遠くへ行っちまう。
English: Whenever I find a girl I like, they always run away!

Japanese: こないだはチベットまで追いかけた ケド‥‥今度は、刑務所だぜ。
English: I even chased one of them to Tibet... Next it's going to be prison, I guess.

Japanese: しかたねえ。あの刑事の サイフでも、盗んでみるか？
English: ...I'll steal that detective's wallet. That'll get me locked up for sure.

Japanese: いや。‥‥カオからして 不景気だったしなあ、あの刑事‥‥
English: ...Nah. I can't do that to someone who looks like he's down on his luck.

Japanese: ‥‥好きなコト言ってるッス。
English: ...He's just talking to himself.

Japanese: シッ！　ダマって聞くのだ。
English: Shh! Be quiet and listen!

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥マズかったよなあ。 オレが、あんなコトしなければ‥‥
English: I knew it... I shouldn't have done that! I blew it again...

Japanese: （‥‥あんなコト‥‥？）
English: ("Done that"? What did he do, I wonder...)

Japanese: コラ！　ちょっとアンタ！ ‥‥今のヒトコト、異議ありッス！
English: Hey! You! About what you just said... I got an objection!

Japanese: ななな、な、なんだなんだ！ 御剣ィ！　き、キタネエぞ！
English: Wh-Wh-Wh-What the...!? Ed-Edgey! You dirty rat!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥バカ刑事め）
English: *glare* (Gumshoe... You oaf...!)

Japanese: も‥‥申しわけねッス！
English: I-I'm sorry, sir!

Japanese: 気がついたら、思わず叫んで いたッス。‥‥“異議あり”って。
English: Before I knew it, I was shouting out, "Objection!".

Japanese: それもハラの底から、大声で。 人さし指までつきつけて！
English: And in a loud, commanding voice, too! I even pointed with my pointer finger!

Japanese: ‥‥裁判の見すぎだ。
English: ...You've watched too many trials.

Japanese: あいすまねッス。
English: I'm sorry!

Japanese: しかたないな、矢張。 ‥‥ハナシを聞かせてもらおうか。
English: OK, Larry, the jig is up. What have you got to say for yourself?

Japanese: ううううう‥‥。
English: Ungh...

Japanese: ワラぶきの屋根が、すっぽり 雪化粧をしている。
English: The straw roof is totally covered with freshly fallen powdery snow.

Japanese: 自分、こういうフンイキ、 好きッスねー。
English: Wow, makes a nice image, doesn't it? I always did like the rustic look.

Japanese: 《極楽庵》‥‥。あるイミ、 ふさわしい名かもしれぬ。
English: "Heavenly Hall"... I suppose it's an appropriate name in some sense.

Japanese: たしかになー。
English: You got that right.

Japanese: 《極楽庵》‥‥ 『キワめてタノしいオレ』だもん。
English: Look at that bridge up there. This place has to be heaven, because that thing is hell.

Japanese: おぼえておくといい、矢張。
English: You would do well to remember, Larry...

Japanese: 《庵》は《オレ》とは読まない。
English: ...that this is a sacred place to some people.

Japanese: ‥‥なんなのだ。この イタイタしいかざりは。
English: What is this festive-looking ornament?

Japanese: 思い出すよなー、小学校のころの 《おたのしみ会》。
English: It reminds me of art class in grade school.

Japanese: クラスのみんなで、キレイに かざりつけしてさあ。
English: We used to have a lot of fun decorating the classroom with origami. Remember?

Japanese: む、むゥ‥‥‥
English: W-Well, I...

Japanese: あ。そういえば、オマエは ダメだったよなー、そういうの。
English: Oh, yeah... You were never any good at it.

Japanese: え。そうなんスか？
English: Really?

Japanese: コイツ、ブキヨウでさあ。 折り紙で、ツルも折れねえの。
English: Yeah, this guy was so bad, he couldn't even fold a dollar, let alone a crane.

Japanese: みんなからなぐさめられて、 クチビルぶるぶるフルわせてたな。
English: Everyone tried to comfort him, but he would just sit there sobbing.

Japanese: へええ、それは意外な一面
English: Really? I never would have expected that.

Japanese: だまれッ！　‥‥あのときの クツジョクは、一生忘れぬ‥‥
English: Be quiet already! I'll never forget the shame of that day!

Japanese: ツルならばッ！　今なら、５ミリの 狂いもなく折りあげてみせる！
English: You want a crane! I can now make a perfect quarter-inch crane without a single flaw!

Japanese: あのな、御剣よォ。
English: You know, Edgey...

Japanese: 折り紙で５ミリの狂いといえば、 そうとうなモンだぞ。
English: Nothing for nothing, but a quarter-inch crane without a single flaw is not easy.

Japanese: そうとうなモンッスよ、御剣検事。
English: Yeah, that's quite a feat, Prosecutor Edgeworth!

Japanese: （こ、これだから‥‥古い友人は ヤッカイなのだ‥‥）
English: (Grr... This is exactly why I hate childhood friends...)

Japanese: これまた、ヒドいラクガキッス。
English: That's a horrible graffiti problem you've got there, pal.

Japanese: アマいなー、刑事さん。 ゲージュツよ、これは。
English: You don't know anything, Detective. This is art!

Japanese: オレの、あやめちゃんに対する 暑苦しいアイってヤツ？
English: It's an expression of my bittersweet love for Iris.

Japanese: ‥‥矢張。芸術とラクガキの ちがいは、なんなのだ？
English: Larry. Do you know what the difference between graffiti and true art is?

Japanese: 作者の自覚、じゃねェか？
English: It's how the artist himself defines it, right?

Japanese: （そんな気もしてきた）
English: (I should've seen that one coming.)

Japanese: そういうイミじゃ、自分の作る ベントウも、リッパな芸術品ッス！
English: Well, if that's the case, then all of my lunchboxes are masterpieces too, pal!

Japanese: おぼろ橋へつづいている。 キョリはあるが、ゆるやかだ。
English: These go all the way to Dusky Bridge. It's a pretty long, but easy, walk.

Japanese: ここへ来るまでに、４回も すべって転んだッス。
English: I fell down 4 times on my way down here, sir!

Japanese: そりゃあ、しかたねえよ 刑事さん。
English: Oh, sorry about that, Detective.

Japanese: オレがここへ来るとき、 バナナを４本、食ったから。
English: I ate four bananas and tossed their peels on the stairs on my way down here...

Japanese: なんとォ！　そうだったッスか！
English: What!? So that's what I was slipping on!

Japanese: 帰りも、気をつけたほうが いいぜ。バナナ。
English: Be careful on the way back. Those deadly banana peels are still out there.

Japanese: ううん‥‥。それは耳よりな 情報ッスね、御剣検事！
English: Hmm... That's a real important piece of information right there, Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: 少し遠いが、おぼろ橋が ハッキリ見える。
English: It's a little far, but I have a pretty clear view of Dusky Bridge from here.

Japanese: あーあ。信じられないよなあ、 ‥‥ホント。
English: Ahh. I still can't believe it! ...Really.

Japanese: ‥‥？　どうした、矢張。
English: What is it, Larry?

Japanese: え！　い、いやいや。 なんでもねえよ！
English: Huh! Err, uhh, nothing. It's nothing!

Japanese: アヤシイそぶりッス！
English: Sounds pretty suspicious to me!

Japanese: （イヤな予感がするな‥‥）
English: (Something about that statement smells... And you know what they say...)

Japanese: これが、吾童川ッス。
English: This is the Eagle River, sir.

Japanese: 流れがはやいため、冬でも 凍ることはないらしいッス。
English: It runs pretty fast, so it doesn't ice over, even in the winter.

Japanese: 凍っていたら、成歩堂も 危なかっただろうな。
English: If it had iced over, Wright would have been in some serious trouble.

Japanese: そりゃそッス！
English: You're right about that!

Japanese: きっと、すごいイキオイで 川下まですべり落ちていったッス！
English: He would've landed on the ice, and slid downstream to who knows where.

Japanese: （‥‥そういうことでは ないのだが‥‥）
English: (...Not exactly what I meant, but alright...)

Japanese: なんなのだ？　この山小屋は。
English: What is this little shack anyway?

Japanese: ‥‥さあな。オレも、きのう 見つけたんだけど。
English: Well, I just discovered it myself yesterday.

Japanese: それに‥‥どうして、 おまえがこんなところに‥‥？
English: And why were you down here in the first place?

Japanese: いやホレ。オレ、画家だからさ。 スケッチする場所を探してたワケ。
English: Err, come on... I'm an artist... I was looking for a good place to sketch.

Japanese: イイじゃねえか、このボロ小屋。 ゲージュツ的でさ。
English: This is a great little place! It's err... artistic.

Japanese: しずかで、サムくて、電気もつかず 今にもくずれそうで。
English: It's quiet, it's cold, it's got no power, and it looks like it's about to collapse.

Japanese: ‥‥自分のアパートを ホウフツとさせるッス。
English: ...Sounds a lot like my apartment there, pal.

Japanese: （たしかに、この小屋‥‥だれも 手入れをしていないようだ‥‥）
English: (One thing's for sure... No one is likely to show up and disturb you here...)

Japanese: なんか、アツいの飲むだろ。 お湯でいいか？
English: So can I get you something to drink? Some hot water, maybe?

Japanese: ううう‥‥すっかり、 なじんでるッス。
English: ...He's getting all buddy-buddy on us, sir.

Japanese: ‥‥なあ、御剣。
English: ...Listen to me, Edgey.

Japanese: あやめちゃんのコト‥‥ ゼッタイ、助けてくれよな！
English: You've gotta do this... You've gotta save Iris!

Japanese: なぜ、彼女が無実だと？ ‥‥“カワイイから”か？
English: Why are you so sure she's innocent? Because she's cute?

Japanese: バカ言え！　そんなんじゃねえよ！ 決めたんだ、オレ。
English: Watch your mouth! Anyway, I've made up my mind about it!

Japanese: ‥‥あの子と、ケッコンする。
English: I'm going to marry that girl!

Japanese: ‥‥あの。
English: Umm...

Japanese: 御剣検事が知らんプリしてるから、 かわりに自分が聞くッス。
English: Mr. Edgeworth is pretending he didn't hear you, so I'm gonna ask for him.

Japanese: アンタ、あの子に プロポーズでもしたッスか？
English: Have you already asked this girl to marry you?

Japanese: いやいや。 オレを見る目でわかるんだよ！
English: No, no. Not yet. But I can tell how she feels by the look in her eyes!

Japanese: “この子、オレにキョーミがある” ‥‥ってさあ！
English: She's got this "I really want this man to carry me over the threshold" look!

Japanese: きっと、成歩堂もビックリするぜ！
English: I'm sure Nick would be surprised!

Japanese: あんなかわいい子が、オレの おヨメさんになるって知ったら！
English: He'd never imagine that I could marry such a beautiful girl like that!

Japanese: たしかに‥‥ ビックリするだろうな。
English: Something tells me he would be shocked, indeed.

Japanese: ‥‥だからさァ。あんまり ムチャしてほしくないよな、彼女。
English: That's why I didn't want her to do anything dangerous.

Japanese: ケガでもしたら、どうするんだよ。
English: I mean, what am I gonna do if she gets hurt?

Japanese: さっきから、ナニを言ってるッス？ このオトコは‥‥
English: What is this guy trying to say? He lost me about a mile back...

Japanese: むう‥‥それを本当に 知りたいのであれば‥‥
English: Hmm... If we really want to know the answer to that...

Japanese: （証言台に引きずり出すしか ないようだな‥‥）
English: (...we're going to have to drag him onto the witness stand.)

Japanese: そういえば‥‥さっきは 聞かせてもらえなかったな。
English: Come to think of it, you still haven't answered my question.

Japanese: ‥‥ゆうべ、おまえが どこで、何をしていたか‥‥？
English: Where were you and what were you doing last night?

Japanese: オマエさァ‥‥ 他に話題はないのかよ？
English: Aww, man... Don't you have anything else to talk about?

Japanese: そんなコトだから、オレみたいな モテモテになれねえんだよ。
English: With that kind of attitude, you'll never be a ladies' man like me...

Japanese: わ、わかったよ！ そんな目でニラむなって。
English: O-OK, OK! Chill out with those scary eyes! I got it!

Japanese: ‥‥じつはさァ。 オレ、ゆうべ‥‥
English: If you really wanna know, last night...

Japanese: トンでもねえモノを 見ちまったんだよ。
English: ...I saw something incredible.

Japanese: “トンでもないモノ”‥‥？
English: "Something incredible"?

Japanese: ‥‥まあまあ。いいじゃねえか、 そんなコト。どうだってよォ。
English: ...Yeah, yeah. But let's not talk about that now.

Japanese: それより、なんだ。 ムカシの話でもしようぜ。
English: Let's talk about the good old days. What do you say?

Japanese: ホレ。今、アツぅいお湯、 飲ましてやるからさぁ。
English: Come on, I'll pour you a nice cup of hot water.

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥）
English: (Why hasn't he realized...)

Japanese: （ムカシからコイツには、 煮え湯を飲まされてきたっけ）
English: (...that I absolutely despise talking about the "good old days", especially with him?)

Japanese: ゆうべ‥‥おまえは いったい、何を見たのだ？
English: What exactly did you see last night?

Japanese: いやあ‥‥。 ちょっと、それはなァ‥‥
English: Well... It's kinda hard to say...

Japanese: なんて言うかさ。 見たモノもアレなんだけど‥‥
English: It's one of those things... Ya know what I mean...?

Japanese: それを見た状況ってのもちょっと、 アレなモンだからさ。
English: It's like... It's not so much "what" I saw, as it's the situation in general...

Japanese: わかるだろ？　トモダチなら 笑って聞き流せよ。はははははは。
English: You see what I'm getting at? Whew, that's a relief. Thanks, buddy. Ha ha ha ha ha.

Japanese: （ヤッカイな錠ごと、コイツを パリンと粉砕してやりたい‥‥）
English: (I have the strangest urge to smash this guy against those big, nasty locks...)

Japanese: 矢張！　キサマはゆうべ、 この極楽庵にいたのだな？
English: So you were here in Heavenly Hall last night, were you not, Larry?

Japanese: そして、おぼろ橋への落雷を 目撃した！
English: And you saw the lightning hit Dusky Bridge, didn't you?

Japanese: ご、ごめんよお‥‥
English: S-Sorry, Edgey...

Japanese: 見下げ果てたオトコッス！ 婦女子をキョーハクするなんて‥‥
English: Sorry doesn't cut it, you scumbag! Threatening a young lady like that...

Japanese: うううううう‥‥
English: *gulp*...

Japanese: あれ。
English: Wait, hold up.

Japanese: ‥‥なんス？
English: ...What now, pal?

Japanese: なんだよ、さっきから！ キョーハク、キョーハクってよォ！
English: What are you talking about!? What "threatening" stuff!?

Japanese: だから！　アンタは、あやめさんの 秘密をネタに、脅迫状を‥‥
English: I'll tell you what! You tried to scare Iris by threatening to expose her secret, pal!

Japanese: コレのどこが脅迫状なんだよ！
English: What do you mean threaten!? When did I threaten her!?

Japanese: 『あなたの《秘密》を 知られたくなければ』‥‥。
English: "...unless you want your 'secret' to be exposed..."

Japanese: ‥‥どこから見ても、 リッパな脅迫状じゃないッスか！
English: That sure sounds like a threat to me, pal! Blackmail, in fact!

Japanese: バカ言え！　ラブレターだよ！ 恋文！　‥‥決まってるだろ！
English: Gimme a break! It's a love letter! Haven't you ever been in love!?

Japanese: な‥‥‥なんだと‥‥？
English: Wh-What did you just say!?

Japanese: 凍てつく冬山の雪すらもとかす、 オレの暑苦しいアイだよ！
English: My love for her burns so hotly, it could melt all the snow on this mountain!

Japanese: し、しかし‥‥それでは！ この《秘密》というのはなんだ！
English: O-Oh? Then what is this secret you mention!?

Japanese: あやめちゃんの《秘密》‥‥？ そんなの、決まってるだろ。
English: Come on, Edgey... Don't you get it?

Japanese: “あやめちゃんとオレの、おアツい カンケイ”しか、ねえだろうが！
English: I'm talking about the secret love between her and me!

Japanese: ‥‥あやめちゃんもさ。毘忌尼の オバサンに知られちゃコマるだろ？
English: Obviously she wouldn't want old lady Bikini to know about it, right...?

Japanese: こぉんな、アマずっぱくて ホットな《秘密》をさァ！
English: About our... hot and sour... bittersweet love affair!

Japanese: ラブレターなのに、なぜ こんな茶封筒なのだッ！
English: Alright, then why did you send a love letter in a business- like manila envelope!?

Japanese: しかたねェだろ！ 他になかったんだからさァ！
English: Give me a break! It's not my fault I didn't have any other envelopes! Yeesh!

Japanese: じゃア、なんで急に あやまったッスか！
English: Then why were you so quick to apologize, pal!?

Japanese: だって！　御剣の目が コワかったんだもんよォ！
English: It's cuz Edgey gave me that scary look of his!

Japanese: どうした？　御剣。 ダマりこんじゃって。
English: What's wrong, Edgey? Why are you so quiet all of a sudden?

Japanese: これが‥‥たいそうな錠の コタエだと言うのか‥‥？
English: That's it? That's all those huge locks were about?

Japanese: あ。あまりに、ムナしい‥‥。
English: I-I don't understand why you were so defensive...

Japanese: ‥‥ま。なんか知らんケド、 アレよな。
English: Well, I dunno either... I guess the thing is...

Japanese: オレに多くをキタイしちゃ イカン、ってコトよ。
English: ...you shouldn't expect too much from a guy like me.

Japanese: ‥‥ホレ。元気だせって！
English: ...Hey, come on. Don't let it get you down!

Japanese: し。しかし、あれッスよ、 御剣検事！
English: B-But Mr. Edgeworth!

Japanese: まだ、コイツは かくしてるコトがあるッス！
English: This guy is still hiding something! I know it!

Japanese: ‥‥なんのことだ？　刑事‥‥
English: What do you mean, Detective?

Japanese: この絵描きは、最初に、 こう言ってたハズッス！
English: Don't forget what this guy said just a minute ago!

Japanese: ‥‥じつはさァ。 オレ、ゆうべ‥‥
English: If you really wanna know, last night...

Japanese: トンでもねえモノを 見ちまったんだよ。
English: ...I saw something incredible.

Japanese: （そ、そういえば‥‥）
English: (Hmm... He's right!)

Japanese: 矢張ィィィ‥‥
English: Larry!

Japanese: なな、なんだよ‥‥その、 ハラをすかした子犬みたいな目は！
English: Wh-What!? Y-You're looking at me like a hungry dog that just found a bone!

Japanese: ゆうべ、キサマが見た “トンでもないもの”‥‥
English: What was this "something incredible" you saw last night...?

Japanese: ‥‥最後に、その話を 聞かせてもらおうかッ！
English: ...You're going to tell me, Larry! One way or another!

Japanese: だ‥‥だから、言ったろ？
English: I... I already told you, didn't I?

Japanese: おぼろ橋に、カミナリが 落ちたところを見た、って。
English: I saw lightning strike Dusky Bridge!

Japanese: ‥‥さっきはそれで ナットクしたが‥‥
English: Yes, and I believe it was the incredible sight you saw.

Japanese: どう考えても、不自然なのだ。
English: But now that I think about it, something doesn't quite ring true.

Japanese: な、ナニがよ？
English: Wh-What doesn't?

Japanese: もし、本当にそれだけならば‥‥ ココロにカギをかけるまでもない！
English: If that's all there is to your story, your heart wouldn't have had all those locks!

Japanese: そう‥‥
English: Yes, Larry...

Japanese: キサマは、ゆうべ！ ‥‥もっとスゴいものを見たのだ。
English: I believe you saw something last night. Something more incredible than lightning.

Japanese: な、なんでだよ！　どーしてだよ！ ワケわかんねーよ！
English: Wh-What!? Wh-When!? Wh-Where!? Wh-Why!? H-How!?

Japanese: コラ！　アンタ！
English: Hey! What do you think you're doing!?

Japanese: 御剣検事にかくしゴトをしたら、 もー、タイホッス！
English: If you hide anything from Mr. Edgeworth, I'll arrest you on the spot, pal!
Ah...

Japanese: うおおおおおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: Aaaaaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: ‥‥ムグオオオオオオオオオオッ！
English: ...Nooooooooooooooooooo!

Japanese: ど、どうしたッスか？
English: Wh-What's wrong, sir?

Japanese: ま、また‥‥最初から やりなおせ、というのか‥‥？
English: D-Does this mean I have to do it all over again?

Japanese: な、なんかオマエの視線‥‥ ミョーにアツいぜ！
English: Wh-Why are you glaring at me like I'm next to be hit by a bolt of lightning!?

Japanese: （‥‥こ、コイツはもう、 キリがないな‥‥）
English: (I've just about had it with this harlequin...)

Japanese: （すべてのコタエを 引きずり出すためには‥‥）
English: (If I really want to drag the truth out of him...)

Japanese: （コイツ自身を引きずり出すしか ないようだ‥‥証人席に！）
English: (...I'll just have to drag him to the witness stand!)

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: お、マヨイちゃん。
English: Oh, it's Maya.

Japanese: そういえば、成歩堂のヤツ、 あの子を助けようとしたんだよな。
English: Nick was trying to go save her, you know.

Japanese: なのに、自分で勝手に橋から 落っこちやがって。
English: But instead, he wound up falling off the bridge.

Japanese: ホント、ムカシから 人騒がせなヤツだったよな。
English: I'm not surprised though. Nick always gets himself into trouble.

Japanese: （このオトコに言われたら 成歩堂も成仏できまい‥‥）
English: (Well if that isn't the pot calling the kettle black...)

Japanese: マヨイちゃんも、カゼひいて なきゃ、いいけどなあ‥‥
English: I just hope that Maya doesn't catch a cold, too...

Japanese: たしかに、それはシンパイだな‥‥
English: ...Or something worse.

Japanese: この子！　ハルミちゃん、って いったっけ。
English: Oh, hey! It's Pearl!

Japanese: ムカシつきあっていたハルミより、 ずーっとカワイイんだよなァ。
English: But she's way cuter than this Pearl I used to go out with... Like, in a kid-cute way, yeah?

Japanese: じつは‥‥今朝から、 彼女の姿が見えないのだ。
English: Yes, well... Apparently she hasn't been seen the whole morning.

Japanese: な‥‥なんだって‥‥？ ‥‥御剣ィィッ！
English: Wh-What did you say!? Edgey!

Japanese: オマエがついていながら‥‥ ナニやってんだよ！
English: Why are you wasting your time with me then!? What's wrong with you!?

Japanese: え。
English: Excuse me?

Japanese: 彼女にナニかあったら‥‥ オレは、オマエをユルさねえ。
English: If anything happens to her, I'll never forgive you!

Japanese: ‥‥一方的に 言われちまったッスね。
English: ...Wow, I guess he's right for a change, sir.

Japanese: （なんだか、私が悪いような 気がしてきた‥‥）
English: (Now I feel like I'm the bad guy in all of this...)

Japanese: え、エリス先生が 持っていたツエだ‥‥
English: That's the staff that Ms. Elise was always carrying...

Japanese: せ、せんせえよォォ‥‥
English: M-Ms. Elise... Whyyyy...?

Japanese: ‥‥アレ。
English: ...Huh?

Japanese: どうした？　矢張。
English: What is it, Larry?

Japanese: なんか、このツエ‥‥ ナニか、足りないような‥‥
English: There's something missing from this staff...
(There's something missing...?)

Japanese: ～事件当夜のこと～
English: -- The Night of the Crime --

Japanese: ナニがなんでも聞かせてもらおう。 ゆうべ、おまえが“見た”こと‥‥
English: Alright, now you're going to tell me what you really saw last night...

Japanese: お。キアイ入ってるなー、御剣。 ‥‥わかったよ！　話すって。
English: Whoa. You're really upset, aren't you, Edgey? OK, I'll talk!

Japanese: ‥‥え。 （イキナリ解決、か‥‥？）
English: ...Huh? (That was a bit too easy...)

Japanese: イヤ、あの晩さ。すげェモン、 見ちまったワケよ！
English: Yeah, anyway! It was awesome! Never seen anything like it!

Japanese: 夜の１０時ごろからさァ、 カミナリが鳴り出しただろ。
English: At around 10 o'clock last night, it started thundering.

Japanese: オレ、ウトウトしてたワケよ。 ‥‥どれぐらいたったかなあ。
English: I'd been sleeping... I'm not sure for how long.

Japanese: いきなり‥‥ピシャーン！ もお、目の前がまっ白になって。
English: Suddenly... ZING! The world in front of me went white!

Japanese: ありゃァ、ナオミの ビンタよりスゴかったね。
English: Like I'd just been slapped in the face by my old girlfriend, Naomi!

Japanese: ‥‥それで？
English: ...And then?

Japanese: そしたらさ！ 燃えてるワケよ。あの橋がさァ！
English: And then... It was on fire! The bridge was on fire!

Japanese: おぼろ橋が‥‥燃えた！ それを目撃した、というのか‥‥
English: Dusky Bridge caught on fire!? Are you saying you saw it with your own eyes?

Japanese: なんだよ！　正直に話してるのに、 その、コワい目つきはよォ！
English: Hey! Why are you giving me the evil eye!? I'm telling the truth!

Japanese: （なにぶん、《さいころ錠》が ３つも残っているからな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... There are still 3 Psycholocks remaining...)

Japanese: （‥‥まだ、何かかくしている、 というコトになる‥‥）
English: (That means he's still trying to hide something...)

Japanese: ところで、矢張‥‥。 おまえ、それをどこで見たんだ？
English: By the way, Larry... Where were you when you saw that happen?

Japanese: ど、ど、ど、ど、どこ、って‥‥ そ、そ、そりゃ、どういうイミよ！
English: Wh-Wh-Wh-Where you say? Wh-Wh-What do you mean!?

Japanese: 深いイミなどない。 ‥‥質問に答えてもらおうか。
English: What do you mean, "What do I mean"!? ...Just answer the question!

Japanese: モチロン、葉桜院の‥‥ 自分の部屋から見たんだよ！
English: I-I was in my own room... by the Main Hall!

Japanese: キマってるじゃねえか！
English: Where else would I be!?

Japanese: ‥‥あいかわらず、 わかりやすいオトコだ。
English: As usual, you're as transparent as an empty jelly jar.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥モンダイは そっちにあったようだな‥‥
English: The problem, I suspect, lies there...

Japanese: そ、そ、そ、そ、そっち、って‥‥ そ、そ、そりゃ、どういうイミよ！
English: Th-Th-Th-Th-There? Wh-Wh-Wh-Wh-Whaddya mean th-th-there?

Japanese: おぼろ橋への落雷‥‥おまえが それを、部屋から見たはずがない！
English: It's impossible for you to have seen lightning strike Dusky Bridge from your room!

Japanese: これを見ろ！　矢張ッ！
English: Look at this, Larry!

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥ま。そんなコトよりホラ。 お湯でも飲めよ。
English: Hey, forget about that. Try drinking your hot water.

Japanese: さめちまったら、ただの水だぞ。
English: If you don't drink it fast it's going to get cold.

Japanese: む‥‥すまない。
English: Oh, sorry...

Japanese: （どうやら、さめきっている らしいな‥‥お湯も、証拠も）
English: (It's cold alright... The water... And my evidence.)

Japanese: どうだ？　落ちついたか。
English: Well? Have you calmed down yet?

Japanese: ああ‥‥もう一度、考えてみよう。
English: Um, yes... I'm going to think it over again.

Japanese: だ、だからさァ‥‥。 考えるまでもねえって。
English: H-Hey, I'm telling you... there's nothing to think over!

Japanese: オレ、ゆうべは葉桜院で 花占いやってただけだぜ！
English: I was in my room doing "she loves me, she loves me not" with a bunch of flowers.

Japanese: これは、現場付近の上面図だ。 ‥‥葉桜院のまわりを見ろ、矢張。
English: This is a map of the area. Take a look around the vicinity of Hazakura Temple.

Japanese: ‥‥ど、どういうコトよ。
English: Wh-What am I looking for?

Japanese: セツメイするまでもなかろう。
English: I think that should be fairly obvious.

Japanese: 葉桜院は、森でおおわれている。 ここから、橋が見えるハズがない。
English: The Main Hall is surrounded by trees and it's impossible to see the bridge from here.

Japanese: な‥‥‥なんだよそれ！ 先に言えよな、そういうコトは！
English: What...!? Why didn't you tell me that before!?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥で、どうだ？
English: ...Well? How about it?

Japanese: ナニがよ？
English: How about what?

Japanese: しゃべる気になったか？
English: Do you feel like talking now?

Japanese: ナニをよ？
English: About what?

Japanese: （どうやら、まだアマいようだ）
English: (It looks like it won't be that easy after all...)

Japanese: ‥‥やむを得ない。 次の段階へ進むとしよう。
English: ...You leave me no choice. I'll have to move on to the next step.

Japanese: おまえは葉桜院にはいなかった。 それならば‥‥“どこにいたか？”
English: You weren't in your room at the temple. So then... Where were you?

Japanese: そ、そ、そ、そんなの‥‥ わかるワケないぜ！
English: Y-Y-You don't know that I wasn't in my room!

Japanese: （矢張がいた場所‥‥そして、 なぜ、そこにいたか‥‥？）
English: (So where was Larry, and why was he there...?)

Japanese: （先まで読みきってしまえば‥‥ コタエは、もうアキラカだろう）
English: (If I've read the situation up to this point correctly, the answer is fairly obvious.)

Japanese: ‥‥よかろう、試してみようか。
English: ...Very well then. Let's test my theory.

Japanese: おまえは、おぼろ橋への 落雷を目撃した。‥‥この場所で！
English: The place you witnessed lightning striking Dusky Bridge from was... here!

Japanese: おまえは、ここにいた！ ‥‥どうだ、矢張！
English: This is where you were! Well, what do you think, Larry!?

Japanese: まあ、ぼちぼち‥‥かな。
English: Oh... Not much I guess.

Japanese: スマン。何がだ？
English: I'm sorry, but what kind of answer is that?

Japanese: ナニ、って‥‥世間話だろ？ 『どうだ最近？』って聞くから‥‥
English: Well, you were just making small talk, right? Like "How are you doing?" or something.
...Larry.

Japanese: このカオが、世間話を しているように見えるか？
English: Look at the expression on my face. Do I look like I was just making small talk?

Japanese: ‥‥ま。正直、 見えないケドな。はははははは。
English: To be honest? No... I guess not. Ha ha ha ha ha...

Japanese: （私としたことが‥‥ 考えが足りなかったようだ）
English: (It appears I didn't think it through carefully enough.)

Japanese: おまえが落雷を見た場所‥‥ もちろん、この《極楽庵》だッ！
English: The place you saw the lightning strike from was naturally Heavenly Hall!

Japanese: ‥‥なな、なんでオレが こんなボロ小屋に‥‥！
English: ...Wh-Why would I be hanging out in this old shack?

Japanese: サムくて、電気もつかず、 今にもくずれそうだってのに‥‥
English: It's freezing cold, there's no electricity, and it could fall apart at any minute...

Japanese: 矢張。‥‥そもそも、 なぜ、キサマは知っているのだ？
English: Larry... How do you know that anyway?

Japanese: “電気がつかない”コトを。 ‥‥今はまだ、そんなに暗くない。
English: How do you know there's no electricity? After all, it's not that dark yet.

Japanese: あ、ああ‥‥ッ！
English: Ah... Uh-oh!

Japanese: つまり、これで証明されたわけだ。
English: In other words, you have just provided evidence to prove my theory...

Japanese: キサマは‥‥暗くなってから、 ここへ来たことがあるのだッ！
English: My theory that you've at least once in your life visited Heavenly Hall after sunset!

Japanese: さ‥‥さすがだな、御剣。 成歩堂とは、ヒトアジちがうぜ‥‥
English: I have to admit I'm impressed, Edgey! You're in a totally different league from Nick.

Japanese: ‥‥さあ、聞かせてもらおうか。
English: ...That's nice. Now tell me.

Japanese: ゆうべ、キサマはなぜ、こんな サムい山小屋にいたのか‥‥？
English: What were you doing at this cold little shack last night?

Japanese: それは‥‥その。 バリケードな問題なワケよ！
English: That's what you might call... A "Fair de Core".

Japanese: “デリケート”だ。
English: I think you mean "affaire de coeur".

Japanese: ‥‥もしかして、キサマは‥‥ だれかを待っていたのではないか？
English: Could it be you were... waiting for someone?

Japanese: うおおおおおおッ！
English: Ooooh nooo!

Japanese: ‥‥お、オマエって‥‥ ホント、コワいヤツだな‥‥
English: Y-You really are one scary guy, you know...

Japanese: ゆうべ‥‥キサマはここで、 この人物が来るのを待っていた！
English: I believe that last night, you were waiting for this person to come meet you!

Japanese: ‥‥どうだ、矢張！ キサマは、この人物を‥‥
English: ...Well, Larry!? Was it THIS person...?

Japanese: ‥‥オマエさー。
English: ...You know what?

Japanese: ヒトに指をつきつけるの、 法廷だけにしておけよな。
English: You should save your finger pointing for the courtroom.
Tsk...!

Japanese: だから《シゴト人間》とか言われる ワケよ。‥‥オレを見習えって！
English: It's no wonder people call you a workaholic. You work too hard! You should be like me!

Japanese: （おまえは《ダメ人間》と 言われてるハズだが‥‥）
English: (Well, I'd rather "work too hard" than "hardly work" like a loafaholic like you...)

Japanese: とにかく‥‥キサマは だれか待っていたはずなのだ！
English: In any case... I know you were waiting for someone!

Japanese: だ、だから‥‥そいつはその。 デリカテッセンな問題、なワケよ！
English: Yeah, but... It's like I said. It's an "affaire de guerre".

Japanese: “デリケート”だ。 ‥‥たぶん。
English: No, Larry. You mean "affaire de coeur". At least I think you do.

Japanese: こんなヒドい場所で、キサマが 待つ相手など、ひとりしかいない。
English: There's only one person you'd wait for in a horrible place like this, Larry.

Japanese: さっきから‥‥ “キサマ”はないだろ‥‥
English: I told you before... Don't call me "Larry"...

Japanese: 待っていたのは‥‥そう。 あやめさんだッ！
English: The person you were waiting for was... Iris!

Japanese: うううう‥‥なんだか サムくなってきたよォ、御剣ィ。
English: Ohh... Suddenly I feel cold all over, Edgey.

Japanese: 私の視線が、かなり冷たいからな。
English: No doubt because of my chilly glare.

Japanese: そりゃ、オレ‥‥あやめちゃんに ゾッコンだけどよォ‥‥
English: So you think I got the hots for Sister Iris, huh...?

Japanese: な、なんか、証拠でもあるのかよ！
English: D-Do you have some kind of evidence!?

Japanese: オレがここで彼女を待っていた、 っていう証拠だよ！
English: You got something that proves I was waiting for her!? Or are you just guessing?

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥これで トドメが刺せそうだな‥‥）
English: (This is where I draw the line and end this ridiculous little game...)

Japanese: キサマが、あやめさんを 待っていたという証拠なら‥‥
English: Here's the evidence that you were waiting for Iris...

Japanese: き、気のせいだろうか？
English: I-Is it just me?

Japanese: なんだか私も、急に サムくなってきたのだが‥‥
English: Now, I'm the one that suddenly feels cold...

Japanese: オレの冷笑も、かなり 冷えこむってウワサだからな。
English: Maybe it's because of my cold laugh?

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥つまらない ところでミスをしたようだ‥‥）
English: (Grr... I hate making such stupid mistakes...)

Japanese: ホントにあるのかよ、御剣！ 証拠なんてさァ。
English: Do you really have the evidence you say you do, Edgey?

Japanese: ‥‥コイツが、その証拠だ。
English: ...Here's your evidence.

Japanese: キサマは、彼女を呼びだしたのだ。 この、くしゃくしゃの脅迫状で！
English: You called her to this spot with a pathetic blackmail letter!

Japanese: う‥‥うわわわ、それ！ 返せッ！　ハズカシイじゃねえか！
English: Oh! H-Hey! G-G-Give that back! You're embarrassing me!

Japanese: なんでオマエが 持ってるんだよ、コレ！
English: What are you doing with that anyway!?

Japanese: そんなコトは、問題ではない‥‥。 見そこなったぞ、矢張。
English: That's not important! ...I misjudged you, Larry.

Japanese: な、ナニがよ‥‥？
English: What do you mean...?

Japanese: かよわい女性の弱みにつけこんで、 恥ずかしくないのかッ！
English: Taking advantage of a woman's frailty like that! You should be ashamed of yourself!

Japanese: う‥‥‥うううう‥‥
English: Ohhh... OHHHH!

Japanese: だいいち、なんだ！　この手紙の アタマの《全略》というのは！
English: First of all, what's this at the top of the letter? It says, "Salutation here"!

Japanese: ‥‥だ、だって。《手紙の書き方》 って本に、『そう書け』って‥‥
English: W-Well that's what it said in that book, "Letter Writing for Dummies"!

Japanese: それは《前略》だッ！
English: You're not supposed to actually write that!

Japanese: ゼンブ略したら、もう 書くことがないではないかッ！
English: That's where you're supposed to write, "Dear Iris,"!

Japanese: うわああああああああああああん！ ゴメンよおおおおおおおおおおお！
English: WAAAAAAAAH!!! I'M SO SOOORRY!

Japanese: （今はまだ、証拠が 足りないのかもしれない）
English: (It looks like I still don't have enough information.)

Japanese: （もう少し調査をして、 材料を集めるべきか‥‥）
English: (I need to investigate further and gather some more evidence...)

Japanese: ‥みつるぎさま‥
English: ...Mr. Edgeworth...

Japanese: ‥これ以上つづけると、 ココロがこわれてしまいます‥
English: ...If you push yourself any more, your soul will shatter...

Japanese: ‥もう一度、冷静になって 考えなおしましょう‥
English: ...Please calm down, collect your thoughts, and try again...

Japanese: （むぅぅ‥‥ シッパイしすぎたか‥‥）
English: (Blast it...! I've made too many errors...!)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 葉桜院　山門
English: February 8 Hazakura Temple Main Gate

Japanese: ‥‥おや。 矢張の姿が見えないな。
English: ...Hmm. I don't see Larry anywhere.

Japanese: きっと、ワレワレに 恐れをなしたッスね！
English: Maybe we scared the poor kid away!

Japanese: （アイツは、ココロに 《さいころ錠》を抱えていた）
English: (His heart was shut tight with a Psycholock.)

Japanese: （いずれ、探し出さねば なるまいな。‥‥メンドウだが）
English: (I guess I'll have to look for him now... What a thorn in my side.)
